chapters.titleEn: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic

chapters.titleEn: Chapter 36 — Toxicology: Substance Abuse and Poisoning

chapters.titleEn: Chapter 8 — Vascular Access and Medication Administration

chapters.titleEn: Chapter 12 — Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada

chapters.titleZh: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury
chapters.titleEn: Chapter 20 — Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury

questions.questionZh: 下列哪一项最能说明 airway、oxygenation、ventilation 三者的关系？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best reflects the relationship among airway, oxygenation, and ventilation?

options.zh: 它们完全是同一个概念
options.en: They are exactly the same concept

options.zh: 只要有氧气瓶就代表 ventilation 足够
options.en: Having an oxygen tank means ventilation is adequate

options.zh: 它们彼此相关，但不是完全相同的问题
options.en: They are closely related, but not identical problems

options.zh: 只要病人能说话，就说明三者都没问题
options.en: If the patient can speak, all three are definitely fine

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 的核心之一，就是分清 airway 通不通、氧气有没有送进去、通气够不够，这三者相关但并不完全相同。
questions.explanationEn: A central learning point in Chapter 13 is that airway patency, oxygenation, and ventilation are related but distinct clinical concepts.

questions.questionZh: Patient assessment 最核心的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central purpose of patient assessment?

options.zh: 把 history、exam、scene 与 ongoing reassessment 串成完整判断流程
options.en: To connect history, exam, scene information, and reassessment into a complete decision process

options.zh: 只记录生命体征
options.en: To record vital signs only

options.zh: 跳过病史直接做所有操作
options.en: To skip history and perform all interventions immediately

options.zh: 只在 hospital 才有意义
options.en: It is only meaningful in hospital settings

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 是整本书的方法论主轴，强调 assessment 是把 scene、病史、查体、持续评估整合起来，而不是做单一动作。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 17 acts as a methodological spine for the book, integrating scene findings, history, physical exam, and ongoing reassessment into a coherent clinical process.

questions.questionZh: 在 airway assessment 中，最危险的错误之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most dangerous errors during airway assessment?

options.zh: 把能说几句话误当成绝对安全
options.en: Assuming a patient is fully safe simply because they can speak a few words

options.zh: 先看病人整体外观
options.en: Looking at the overall presentation first

options.zh: 注意 suction 是否需要
options.en: Recognizing that suction may be needed

options.zh: 区分 airway 与 ventilation 问题
options.en: Distinguishing airway problems from ventilation problems

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 强调 airway 状态可能快速恶化，能说话不代表后续不会崩。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 13 emphasizes that airway status may deteriorate rapidly; the ability to speak does not guarantee ongoing airway safety.

questions.questionZh: Patient assessment 的价值最准确的说法是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best captures the value of patient assessment?

options.zh: 只做一次 initial exam 就够了
options.en: A single initial exam is enough

options.zh: 它是把 scene、history、exam 和 reassessment 串成连续临床判断
options.en: It links scene findings, history, exam, and reassessment into a continuous clinical judgment process

options.zh: 它主要是为了写 paperwork
options.en: It exists mainly for paperwork

options.zh: 它只适用于 trauma
options.en: It only applies to trauma

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 的核心是连续评估，不是只做一个静态检查。
questions.explanationEn: The core of Chapter 17 is dynamic, ongoing assessment—not a one-time static check.

questions.questionZh: Clinical decisions 这一章最强调哪种能力？
questions.questionEn: What ability is most emphasized in the Clinical Decisions chapter?

options.zh: 背出所有标准答案
options.en: Memorizing every standard answer

options.zh: 把知识转化成优先级判断与实际行动
options.en: Turning knowledge into prioritization and practical action

options.zh: 只按感觉做决定
options.en: Making decisions based purely on intuition

options.zh: 完全跳过 reassessment
options.en: Completely skipping reassessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 18 的重点是 critical thinking 和 real-world decision making。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 18 focuses on critical thinking and real-world decision making, not rote memorization alone.

questions.questionZh: 当病人 airway 可能恶化时，正确思维应更接近哪一个？
questions.questionEn: When a patient's airway may deteriorate, which mindset is most appropriate?

options.zh: 只要现在还好，就不用预判下一步
options.en: If the airway is okay now, no anticipation is needed

options.zh: 应提前准备 suction、adjuncts、oxygen 和后续升级方案
options.en: You should anticipate deterioration and prepare suction, adjuncts, oxygen, and escalation options

options.zh: 只靠 pulse oximeter 就足够
options.en: A pulse oximeter alone is sufficient

options.zh: 等完全阻塞后再处理
options.en: Wait until complete obstruction occurs before intervening

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 强调 airway 管理有很强的前瞻性，不能只看眼前一秒。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 13 emphasizes that airway management is anticipatory; you cannot rely only on the current moment.

questions.questionZh: 病史采集中最重要的目标之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most important goals of history taking?

options.zh: 把所有问题都问一遍，不分轻重
options.en: Ask every possible question without prioritization

options.zh: 快速获取能改变判断与处理方向的关键信息
options.en: Rapidly obtain key information that changes clinical reasoning and management

options.zh: 只记录病人主诉，不问细节
options.en: Record only the chief complaint without detail

options.zh: 完全依赖家属，不听病人说话
options.en: Rely entirely on family and ignore the patient

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 15 讲的不是机械提问，而是提炼真正影响 decision-making 的线索。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 15 is not about mechanical questioning; it is about extracting information that affects decision-making.

questions.questionZh: General impression 在 physical examination 里的作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the role of the general impression in physical examination?

options.zh: 没有太大价值，只是形式步骤
options.en: It has little value and is mostly a formal step

options.zh: 它往往在早期就帮助你判断病人的严重程度和整体状态
options.en: It often helps you judge severity and overall status early in the encounter

options.zh: 只适用于 pediatrics
options.en: It only applies to pediatrics

options.zh: 只能在详细查体结束后才做
options.en: It can only be made after a full detailed exam

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 16 强调 general impression 很早就能影响你的优先级判断。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 16 emphasizes that the general impression shapes prioritization early in patient contact.

questions.questionZh: ongoing assessment 的核心价值是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core value of ongoing assessment?

options.zh: 病人评估做一次就结束，不需要重复
options.en: Patient assessment is done once and does not need repetition

options.zh: 持续确认病情是否变化，并据此调整判断与处理
options.en: To continually detect changes in condition and adjust judgment and management accordingly

options.zh: 只在 paperwork 阶段使用
options.en: It is only used during documentation

options.zh: 它只适用于 trauma patient
options.en: It only applies to trauma patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 强调 assessment 是动态的，不是一次性动作。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 17 emphasizes that assessment is dynamic, not a one-time event.

questions.questionZh: Clinical decision-making 最不应该依赖哪一种方式？
questions.questionEn: Which approach should clinical decision-making rely on the least?

options.zh: critical thinking
options.en: Critical thinking

options.zh: assessment findings
options.en: Assessment findings

options.zh: scene context
options.en: Scene context

options.zh: 单纯凭感觉、不整合证据
options.en: Pure gut feeling without integrating evidence

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 18 讲的是把信息整合成 decision，而不是凭模糊直觉乱选。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 18 focuses on integrating information into decisions, not making vague, unsupported guesses.

questions.questionZh: Communication and documentation 为什么是 assessment 链条的一部分？
questions.questionEn: Why are communication and documentation part of the assessment chain?

options.zh: 因为做完评估后这些已经不重要了
options.en: Because they no longer matter once assessment is finished

options.zh: 因为 clinical findings 必须被正确传达和记录，才能形成连续照护与可追溯性
options.en: Because clinical findings must be communicated and documented correctly to support continuity of care and traceability

options.zh: 因为 radio 只是一种形式
options.en: Because radio use is only symbolic

options.zh: 因为 documentation 只是行政任务
options.en: Because documentation is merely administrative work

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 19 让 assessment 真正闭环：不只是看懂病人，还要把内容正确交出去并留痕。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 19 closes the loop: it is not enough to understand the patient; the information must also be communicated and recorded properly.

questions.questionZh: 在 Chapter 13 的框架里，哪一种想法最危险？
questions.questionEn: Within the Chapter 13 framework, which mindset is most dangerous?

options.zh: 提前准备 airway adjuncts 和 suction
options.en: Preparing airway adjuncts and suction in advance

options.zh: 把 airway problem 当成可能快速恶化的问题
options.en: Treating airway problems as issues that may deteriorate rapidly

options.zh: 等到病人完全无法维持气道时再开始想下一步
options.en: Waiting until the patient completely loses airway control before planning the next step

options.zh: 区分氧合与通气问题
options.en: Distinguishing oxygenation from ventilation problems

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 强调 airway 管理必须前瞻，而不是等到完全崩掉才反应。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 13 emphasizes anticipatory airway management rather than waiting for complete failure before acting.

questions.questionZh: Mechanical ventilation 这一章与 Chapter 13 的关系，最准确的是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which best describes the relationship between Mechanical Ventilation and Chapter 13?

options.zh: 它和 Chapter 13 没什么关系
options.en: It is mostly unrelated to Chapter 13

options.zh: 它是在基础 airway/ventilation 支持之上，进入更高级、更持续的通气管理
options.en: It builds on basic airway/ventilation support and moves into more advanced, ongoing ventilatory management

options.zh: 它只讲设备品牌
options.en: It only discusses equipment brands

options.zh: 它只适用于 hospital ICU，不适合 EMS 理解
options.en: It only applies to hospital ICUs and is not relevant to EMS understanding

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 14 是在 Chapter 13 的基础上往更高层级的呼吸支持推进。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 14 extends the principles of Chapter 13 into more advanced forms of respiratory support and ventilator management.

questions.questionZh: 关于敏感病史（sensitive topics），最合适的理解是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: What is the most appropriate understanding of obtaining a history on sensitive topics?

options.zh: 因为敏感，就完全不要问
options.en: Because it is sensitive, it should never be asked about

options.zh: 要用合适的沟通方式获取仍然重要的临床信息
options.en: Important clinical information still needs to be obtained using appropriate communication techniques

options.zh: 只要家属在场就随便问
options.en: As long as family is present, questions can be asked without adaptation

options.zh: 只适用于精神科病人
options.en: It only applies to psychiatric patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 15 不是让你回避敏感问题，而是学会怎么在尊重与临床需要之间平衡。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 15 is not about avoiding sensitive topics entirely, but about balancing respect, trust, and clinical necessity.

questions.questionZh: Physical examination 的局限性提醒我们什么？
questions.questionEn: What does the limitation of physical examination remind us of?

options.zh: 查体没什么用
options.en: Physical exam is of little use

options.zh: 查体必须和 history、scene、vitals 与 reassessment 结合理解
options.en: Physical exam findings must be interpreted together with history, scene information, vitals, and reassessment

options.zh: 只要生命体征正常，就可以忽略其他内容
options.en: If vital signs are normal, everything else can be ignored

options.zh: 只要看起来稳定就不需要查体
options.en: A stable appearance means physical exam is unnecessary

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 16 强调 physical exam 很重要，但从来不是孤立存在的。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 16 stresses that physical examination is important, but never stands alone.

questions.questionZh: 在 EMS 中，radio communication 最重要的价值之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most important values of radio communication in EMS?

options.zh: 只是形式上的流程要求
options.en: It is only a formal requirement

options.zh: 帮助资源协调、院前与接收端沟通，以及提前准备下一步照护
options.en: It supports resource coordination, prehospital-to-receiving-facility communication, and advance preparation for further care

options.zh: 它只适用于大型灾害
options.en: It only matters in major disasters

options.zh: 只在 documentation 结束后才需要
options.en: It is only needed after documentation is completed

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 19 里 communication 不是附属，而是 EMS 连续照护链的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 19, communication is not an accessory; it is part of the EMS chain of care.

questions.questionZh: 在 patient assessment 中，scene assessment 的意义最接近哪一项？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best reflects the role of scene assessment in patient assessment?

options.zh: 只是在到场后看一眼环境
options.en: It is merely taking a quick look at the environment after arrival

options.zh: 它帮助你识别安全、机制、资源需求与病情背景，影响后续整条判断链
options.en: It helps identify safety issues, mechanism, resource needs, and patient context, influencing the entire downstream decision chain

options.zh: 它只适用于创伤现场
options.en: It applies only to trauma scenes

options.zh: 它在病人接触后就不再重要
options.en: It stops mattering once you touch the patient

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 的 scene assessment 不只是看环境，而是决定接下来评估和处理方向的重要起点。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 17, scene assessment is not a superficial glance; it sets the stage for the rest of the assessment and management process.

questions.questionZh: 临床决策里“优先级”最核心的含义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central meaning of prioritization in clinical decision-making?

options.zh: 把所有问题同时处理
options.en: Treating all problems simultaneously

options.zh: 先处理最直接威胁生命或最可能快速恶化的问题
options.en: Addressing the most immediate life threats or rapidly deteriorating problems first

options.zh: 先处理最容易的事
options.en: Doing the easiest tasks first

options.zh: 先做 documentation，再看病人
options.en: Completing documentation before assessing the patient

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 18 的 decision-making 核心，就是区分轻重缓急，而不是平均分配注意力。
questions.explanationEn: A central message in Chapter 18 is that decision-making depends on recognizing urgency and sequencing care appropriately.

questions.questionZh: documentation 在 EMS 中最不应该被理解成什么？
questions.questionEn: What should documentation in EMS never be reduced to?

options.zh: 连续照护的一部分
options.en: A component of continuity of care

options.zh: 法律与专业责任的一部分
options.en: Part of legal and professional accountability

options.zh: 只是烦人的行政工作
options.en: Just annoying administrative work

options.zh: 病人情况与处理过程的可追溯记录
options.en: A traceable record of patient condition and interventions

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 19 把 documentation 放在 clinical + legal + communication 三重意义下，不能把它贬成纯行政。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 19 frames documentation as clinically, legally, and communicatively essential—not as mere bureaucracy.

questions.questionZh: Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury 这一章的核心训练是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central training goal of Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury?

options.zh: 只记住车祸的分类
options.en: Memorizing categories of vehicle collisions only

options.zh: 从受伤机制反推潜在损伤和严重度
options.en: Inferring potential injuries and severity from the mechanism of injury

options.zh: 只在 hospital trauma bay 才有意义
options.en: It only matters in the hospital trauma bay

options.zh: 只看外伤表面大小
options.en: Focusing only on the visible size of injuries

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 20 的灵魂就是：发生了什么，决定你要先怀疑哪里坏了。
questions.explanationEn: The essence of Chapter 20 is that what happened helps predict what may be injured and how severe the situation might be.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Bleeding and Shock 是 trauma 里的超核心章节之一？
questions.questionEn: Why is Bleeding and Shock one of the most critical trauma chapters?

options.zh: 因为它只讲理论，不讲处理
options.en: Because it is purely theoretical

options.zh: 因为出血与灌注失败直接关系到病人能否继续存活
options.en: Because hemorrhage and perfusion failure directly affect whether the patient can survive

options.zh: 因为它只和骨折有关
options.en: Because it only concerns fractures

options.zh: 因为休克只看血压就能判断
options.en: Because shock can be identified by blood pressure alone

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 21 在 trauma 里极关键，因为它连接了出血控制、灌注判断、休克识别与快速转运。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 21 is central to trauma because it links hemorrhage control, perfusion assessment, shock recognition, and transport urgency.

questions.questionZh: Soft-tissue injuries 为什么容易被低估？
questions.questionEn: Why are soft-tissue injuries often underestimated?

options.zh: 因为它们永远不会严重
options.en: Because they are never serious

options.zh: 因为表面损伤外观不一定能反映实际组织损害或并发风险
options.en: Because the visible surface appearance may not reflect true tissue damage or complication risk

options.zh: 因为它们只存在于轻伤病人
options.en: Because they only occur in minor injuries

options.zh: 因为它们不需要 documentation
options.en: Because they do not require documentation

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 22 提醒你，不是外观看起来小就一定轻，软组织伤也可能隐藏严重问题。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 22 reminds you that a seemingly small external wound may still conceal important tissue damage or risk.

questions.questionZh: 在 soft-tissue injuries 里，assessment 的重点之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one key focus of assessment in soft-tissue injuries?

options.zh: 只看有没有流血
options.en: Only checking whether there is bleeding

options.zh: 评估伤口类型、部位、深度、污染风险和相关结构损伤
options.en: Assessing wound type, location, depth, contamination risk, and associated structural injury

options.zh: 只要包起来就好
options.en: Simply covering it is enough

options.zh: 伤口部位不重要
options.en: The location of the wound is unimportant

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 22 的 wound assessment 是系统化的，不只是看有没有血。
questions.explanationEn: Wound assessment in Chapter 22 is systematic, not limited to whether blood is present.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Burns 不应被简单理解成“皮肤问题”？
questions.questionEn: Why should burns not be treated as merely a skin problem?

options.zh: 因为所有烧伤都会自己好
options.en: Because all burns heal on their own

options.zh: 因为烧伤可能带来体液、温度、感染与全身性生理问题
options.en: Because burns can create fluid, temperature, infection, and broader systemic physiological problems

options.zh: 因为烧伤只在儿童危险
options.en: Because burns are only dangerous in children

options.zh: 因为只要面积小就一定不严重
options.en: Because a small visible burn is always minor

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 23 强调烧伤是全身性问题，不能只盯着皮肤表面。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 23 emphasizes that burns can be systemic problems rather than superficial skin injuries only.

questions.questionZh: Burn assessment 最重要的意义之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most important purposes of burn assessment?

options.zh: 只为了记录颜色
options.en: Only to document color

options.zh: 帮助判断严重度、处理需求以及是否需要转送烧伤专科中心
options.en: To judge severity, treatment needs, and whether transfer to a burn specialty centre is needed

options.zh: 只适用于 hospital setting
options.en: It only applies in hospital settings

options.zh: 它不影响 transport decisions
options.en: It does not affect transport decisions

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 23 把 burn assessment 和 management、transfer decision 直接连在一起。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 23 directly links burn assessment with management and transfer decisions.

questions.questionZh: 在 head and face injuries 中，哪种想法最危险？
questions.questionEn: Which mindset is most dangerous in head and face injuries?

options.zh: 病人能说话就一定没有 serious brain injury
options.en: If the patient can talk, there cannot be a serious brain injury

options.zh: 面部与头部伤需要同时考虑 airway、neuro status 和隐匿损伤
options.en: Head and face injuries require simultaneous consideration of airway, neurologic status, and occult injury

options.zh: 眼耳口鼻相关损伤也可能重要
options.en: Eye, ear, oral, and facial structures can all be clinically important

options.zh: 要重视 assessment and management of TBI
options.en: Assessment and management of TBI are important

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 24 的一个关键点，就是 head injury 的危险并不总是跟病人当下是否还能说话成正比。
questions.explanationEn: A key lesson from Chapter 24 is that the ability to speak does not rule out a dangerous head or brain injury.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Spine injuries 这一章不能被简化成“看到就固定”这么简单？
questions.questionEn: Why can the chapter on spine injuries not be reduced to simply 'see it and immobilize it'?

options.zh: 因为脊柱问题只在老年人会发生
options.en: Because spinal problems occur only in older adults

options.zh: 因为它牵涉风险识别、神经状态、机制判断与适当管理，而不是机械化动作
options.en: Because it involves risk recognition, neurologic status, mechanism analysis, and appropriate management rather than a purely mechanical response

options.zh: 因为脊柱永远不重要
options.en: Because the spine is never important

options.zh: 因为只有 CT 才重要
options.en: Because only CT matters

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 25 的重点不是僵硬地把所有人一律处理，而是识别真正的脊柱/脊髓风险并适当管理。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 25 focuses on recognizing true spinal/spinal cord risk and managing it appropriately, not just applying a rigid automatic action.

questions.questionZh: Spinal cord injury 最值得警惕的一点是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most important concerns in spinal cord injury?

options.zh: 只要病人还会动就一定安全
options.en: If the patient can still move, everything is safe

options.zh: 它可能带来严重神经功能损害和后续并发问题
options.en: It can lead to major neurologic impairment and important downstream complications

options.zh: 它不会影响 airway 或 circulation
options.en: It never affects airway or circulation

options.zh: 它只和疼痛有关
options.en: It is only about pain

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 25 不只是讲骨头，而是讲脊髓与神经后果。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 25 is not just about bones; it is about spinal cord consequences and neurologic function.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Thoracic injuries 在 trauma 中危险度很高？
questions.questionEn: Why are thoracic injuries particularly dangerous in trauma?

options.zh: 因为胸部只会影响疼痛感受
options.en: Because the chest only affects pain

options.zh: 因为胸部损伤可能迅速影响呼吸、通气、氧合和循环
options.en: Because chest injuries can rapidly impair breathing, ventilation, oxygenation, and circulation

options.zh: 因为它们总是肉眼很明显
options.en: Because they are always visually obvious

options.zh: 因为只要给氧就一定解决
options.en: Because oxygen alone always solves the problem

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 26 把 thoracic injuries 放在高风险位置，是因为它们常直接威胁 ABC。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 26 treats thoracic injuries as high risk because they often threaten core ABC priorities directly.

questions.questionZh: Abdominal injuries 最让人担心的点之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the greatest concerns with abdominal injuries?

options.zh: 腹部伤永远都能从外观看出来
options.en: Abdominal injuries are always obvious from external appearance

options.zh: 它们可能出现明显的内部损伤和出血，但外观并不夸张
options.en: They may involve significant internal injury and bleeding even when the external appearance is not dramatic

options.zh: 只要病人会说话就没事
options.en: If the patient can speak, it is not serious

options.zh: 腹部伤只和消化有关
options.en: Abdominal injury only concerns digestion

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 27 的一个关键风险就是 hidden injury——看起来不夸张，不代表腹腔里没事。
questions.explanationEn: A core risk highlighted in Chapter 27 is hidden injury: the external exam may not reveal the severity of internal abdominal trauma.

questions.questionZh: Musculoskeletal injuries 这一章最容易被误解成什么？
questions.questionEn: What is a common misconception about musculoskeletal injuries?

options.zh: 只是骨头断了的问题
options.en: That they are only about broken bones

options.zh: 它们也可能牵涉血管、神经、夹层、功能和并发症
options.en: They may also involve vessels, nerves, tissue compartments, function, and complications

options.zh: 它们完全不需要 assessment
options.en: They do not require assessment

options.zh: 只要包扎一下就结束
options.en: A simple wrap is always enough

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 28 不只是 fracture list，而是系统看待 limb injury、function、vascular risk 和 complications。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 28 is not merely a fracture list; it systematically approaches limb injury, function, vascular compromise, and complications.

questions.questionZh: 呼吸急症里最关键的第一步思维是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the first critical thinking step in respiratory emergencies?

options.zh: 先分清 airway problem 还是 breathing problem
options.en: First distinguish whether the problem is primarily airway or breathing

options.zh: 只要病人主诉喘就是 asthma
options.en: Assume any shortness of breath is asthma

options.zh: 马上做最终诊断
options.en: Immediately make a final diagnosis

options.zh: 先写 documentation
options.en: Start documentation first

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 29 强调先分系统问题，再谈具体病因。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 29 emphasizes identifying the system problem first before narrowing the exact cause.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 dyspnea 不能被当成单一诊断？
questions.questionEn: Why should dyspnea not be treated as a single diagnosis?

options.zh: 因为它只是一个症状，背后可能有多种系统病因
options.en: Because it is a symptom with many possible underlying system causes

options.zh: 因为 dyspnea 永远等于 panic attack
options.en: Because dyspnea always means a panic attack

options.zh: 因为 dyspnea 只和肺炎有关
options.en: Because dyspnea only relates to pneumonia

options.zh: 因为只要给氧就能解决所有 dyspnea
options.en: Because oxygen alone solves all dyspnea

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 29 训练的是 differential thinking，不是把呼吸困难直接等同某个病名。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 29 trains differential thinking rather than equating dyspnea with one diagnosis.

questions.questionZh: 在 respiratory assessment 中，哪项最危险？
questions.questionEn: Which is most dangerous during respiratory assessment?

options.zh: 病人看起来还能说话，就认为一定稳定
options.en: Assuming a speaking patient is definitely stable

options.zh: 同时观察 work of breathing
options.en: Observing work of breathing

options.zh: 结合 vitals 与整体外观
options.en: Integrating vitals and overall presentation

options.zh: 持续 reassess
options.en: Reassessing continuously

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 29 里 respiratory compromise 可能快速恶化，不能只因为病人还会说话就掉以轻心。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 29, respiratory compromise may deteriorate quickly, so speech alone does not guarantee stability.

questions.questionZh: Cardiovascular emergencies 这一章最核心的主线之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the core threads of the cardiovascular emergencies chapter?

options.zh: 胸痛、心律、灌注与心脏骤停管理
options.en: Chest pain, rhythm, perfusion, and cardiac arrest management

options.zh: 只看心电图，不看病人
options.en: Looking only at the ECG and not the patient

options.zh: 只在 hospital 才需要考虑 circulation
options.en: Considering circulation only in hospital

options.zh: 把所有 chest pain 当成同一种问题
options.en: Treating all chest pain as the same problem

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 30 是整个 cardiovascular emergency map，不只是某一项检查。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 30 provides a full map of cardiovascular emergencies, not just one test or procedure.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 12-lead ECG 在 Chapter 30 中重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the 12-lead ECG important in Chapter 30?

options.zh: 它能帮助更精细识别心脏问题并影响后续处置
options.en: It helps refine recognition of cardiac problems and can affect downstream management

options.zh: 因为有了 12-lead 就不需要 assessment
options.en: Because once you have a 12-lead, no assessment is needed

options.zh: 因为它只适用于教学，不适用于现场
options.en: Because it is only for teaching, not field use

options.zh: 因为它能替代所有生命体征
options.en: Because it replaces all vital signs

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 30 把 cardiac monitoring 与 12-lead 放在 decision-making 链条里。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 30 places cardiac monitoring and 12-lead ECGs within the decision-making chain.

questions.questionZh: Adult cardiac arrest management 为什么是这一章的中心内容之一？
questions.questionEn: Why is adult cardiac arrest management a central topic in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为它是 EMS 中最高风险、最高优先级场景之一
options.en: Because it is one of the highest-risk, highest-priority scenarios in EMS

options.zh: 因为 cardiac arrest 只在 ICU 会发生
options.en: Because cardiac arrest only occurs in ICUs

options.zh: 因为它只和医生有关
options.en: Because it only concerns physicians

options.zh: 因为一旦 arrest 就无法再做任何判断
options.en: Because no further decisions matter once arrest occurs

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 30 与附录 ACLS 是后续高频回头看的核心模块。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 30 and the ACLS appendix form a major high-frequency review area later on.

questions.questionZh: Neurologic emergencies 最核心的 assessment 价值之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one core assessment value in neurologic emergencies?

options.zh: 识别意识改变、局灶神经缺损与病情严重度
options.en: Recognizing altered consciousness, focal deficits, and severity

options.zh: 只判断病人有没有头痛
options.en: Only judging whether the patient has a headache

options.zh: 把所有 altered mental status 当 intoxication
options.en: Treating all altered mental status as intoxication

options.zh: 不需要查体，只问病史就好
options.en: Skipping physical exam and using history alone

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 31 的神经评估核心是识别脑和神经系统是否出问题。
questions.explanationEn: The core of Chapter 31 is recognizing whether the brain or nervous system is affected.

questions.questionZh: 对 seizure / stroke / altered LOC 这类病人，哪种思路更对？
questions.questionEn: Which mindset is more appropriate for seizure, stroke, or altered LOC patients?

options.zh: 把它们当成需要快速系统评估的高风险神经场景
options.en: Treat them as high-risk neurologic scenarios requiring rapid systematic assessment

options.zh: 只要病人现在安静了就不重要
options.en: If the patient is calm now, it is no longer important

options.zh: 一定是精神问题
options.en: They must be psychiatric problems

options.zh: 只需要等 hospital 处理
options.en: Only the hospital needs to deal with them

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 31 强调神经急症的系统评估和及时处理。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 31 emphasizes systematic and timely assessment of neurologic emergencies.

questions.questionZh: Endocrine emergencies 为什么在院前很重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are endocrine emergencies important in the prehospital setting?

options.zh: 因为代谢和血糖问题能明显改变意识与生命体征
options.en: Because metabolic and glucose problems can significantly alter consciousness and vital signs

options.zh: 因为它们永远不紧急
options.en: Because they are never urgent

options.zh: 因为 endocrine 只和长期门诊有关
options.en: Because endocrine issues only matter in long-term outpatient care

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不会遇到 endocrine patient
options.en: Because paramedics never encounter endocrine patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 32 里的重点之一就是这些病人常表现成“看起来怪怪的急症病人”。
questions.explanationEn: One lesson from Chapter 32 is that endocrine emergencies often present as acutely abnormal patients in the field.

questions.questionZh: 关于 hypoglycemia，一种危险误解是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is a dangerous misunderstanding about hypoglycemia?

options.zh: 把它当成一定不会危及生命的小问题
options.en: Treating it as a harmless minor issue

options.zh: 把它纳入 altered mental status 的 differential
options.en: Including it in the differential for altered mental status

options.zh: 注意及时识别与处理
options.en: Recognizing and managing it promptly

options.zh: 把 metabolic cause 纳入 assessment
options.en: Including metabolic causes in assessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 32 的意义之一就是提醒你代谢问题可以非常急。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 32 reminds you that metabolic problems can become highly urgent.

questions.questionZh: Anaphylaxis 为什么是院前急救里必须非常快识别的问题？
questions.questionEn: Why must anaphylaxis be recognized rapidly in prehospital care?

options.zh: 因为它可能同时威胁 airway、breathing 和 circulation
options.en: Because it can threaten airway, breathing, and circulation at the same time

options.zh: 因为它通常完全不会变严重
options.en: Because it usually never becomes severe

options.zh: 因为它只会导致皮肤痒
options.en: Because it only causes itching

options.zh: 因为等病人到 hospital 再说就好
options.en: Because it can wait until the patient reaches hospital

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 33 的高优先级来自其可能快速演变成致命状态。
questions.explanationEn: The high priority of Chapter 33 comes from how quickly anaphylaxis can become life-threatening.

questions.questionZh: 在 allergic reactions 里，哪种情况最需要你提高警觉？
questions.questionEn: In allergic reactions, which situation should raise the greatest concern?

options.zh: 出现提示全身性反应和进展性恶化的表现
options.en: Signs suggesting a systemic reaction and progressive deterioration

options.zh: 只有局部轻微红疹
options.en: A mild isolated local rash only

options.zh: 病人说以前也过敏过
options.en: The patient says they had allergies before

options.zh: 天气很热
options.en: Hot weather

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 33 要求你识别简单过敏与 anaphylaxis 风险之间的差别。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 33 requires distinguishing simple allergic reactions from anaphylaxis risk.

questions.questionZh: Respiratory pharmacology 在这一章里的价值是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the value of respiratory pharmacology in this chapter?

options.zh: 把药物选择和呼吸病理及症状场景连起来
options.en: To connect medication choices with respiratory pathophysiology and symptom patterns

options.zh: 只背药名列表
options.en: To memorize a list of drug names only

options.zh: 替代所有 assessment
options.en: To replace all assessment

options.zh: 只在考试时有用
options.en: It is only useful for exams

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 29 不是药名背诵，而是把药理和 clinical reasoning 连接起来。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 29 is not just memorizing drug names; it links pharmacology to clinical reasoning.

questions.questionZh: 为什么不能把所有 chest pain 当成同一种 emergency？
questions.questionEn: Why can all chest pain not be treated as the same emergency?

options.zh: 因为病因、风险和处置路径可能不同
options.en: Because the causes, risks, and management pathways may differ

options.zh: 因为胸痛都来自胃
options.en: Because all chest pain comes from the stomach

options.zh: 因为只要病人年轻就不严重
options.en: Because if the patient is young it is never serious

options.zh: 因为 chest pain 不属于 EMS 核心内容
options.en: Because chest pain is not a core EMS topic

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 30 训练的就是 cardiovascular differential 和 risk thinking。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 30 trains cardiovascular differential thinking and risk assessment.

questions.questionZh: General management in neurologic emergencies 为什么不能脱离 reassessment？
questions.questionEn: Why can general management in neurologic emergencies not be separated from reassessment?

options.zh: 因为神经状态可能动态变化，持续评估会影响后续处理
options.en: Because neurologic status can change dynamically and reassessment influences further management

options.zh: 因为 reassessment 只适用于 trauma
options.en: Because reassessment applies only to trauma

options.zh: 因为神经病人状态一旦判断就不会变
options.en: Because neurologic status never changes once assessed

options.zh: 因为 documentation 更重要
options.en: Because documentation is more important

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 31 中 neurologic patient 的趋势变化非常重要。
questions.explanationEn: Trend changes are highly important in neurologic patients in Chapter 31.

questions.questionZh: GI emergencies 这一章最核心的学习目标之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the main learning goals of the GI emergencies chapter?

options.zh: 把所有腹痛当成同一个病
options.en: Treat all abdominal pain as the same condition

options.zh: 建立对 GI 症状、评估与常见病况处理的系统理解
options.en: Build a systematic understanding of GI symptoms, assessment, and management of common conditions

options.zh: 只记 anatomy 名词
options.en: Memorize anatomy terms only

options.zh: 认为 GI 问题都不紧急
options.en: Assume GI problems are never urgent

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 34 的重点是把腹部/消化系统 complaint 系统化，而不是模糊处理。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 34 systematizes GI complaints rather than treating them vaguely.

questions.questionZh: 为什么不能把 abdominal pain 视为简单 complaint？
questions.questionEn: Why should abdominal pain not be treated as a simple complaint?

options.zh: 因为背后可能对应多种严重程度和系统病因
options.en: Because it may reflect multiple causes with different levels of severity

options.zh: 因为腹痛只来自胃
options.en: Because abdominal pain only comes from the stomach

options.zh: 因为只要病人还能走就没事
options.en: Because if the patient can walk, it is not serious

options.zh: 因为 GI complaint 不需要 assessment
options.en: Because GI complaints do not require assessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 34 训练的是 differential thinking，不是见腹痛就套一个病名。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 34 trains differential thinking rather than assigning one label to all abdominal pain.

questions.questionZh: Renal and urologic emergencies 这一章的重点之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one emphasis of the renal and urologic emergencies chapter?

options.zh: 识别泌尿系统 complaint 背后的风险和处理方向
options.en: Recognizing risks and management directions behind renal/urologic complaints

options.zh: 认为这类问题都可以等很久
options.en: Assuming these problems can always wait

options.zh: 只关注实验室结果
options.en: Focusing only on laboratory results

options.zh: 只在住院病人中出现
options.en: Thinking they occur only in admitted patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 35 的价值在于把 renal/urologic complaint 放进院前急救 assessment 里。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 35 places renal and urologic complaints within a prehospital assessment framework.

questions.questionZh: 在 renal / urologic complaint 中，为什么 assessment 仍然很关键？
questions.questionEn: Why is assessment still crucial in renal/urologic complaints?

options.zh: 因为症状与严重度并不总是直接对应
options.en: Because symptoms do not always correspond directly to severity

options.zh: 因为所有泌尿问题都一样
options.en: Because all urinary problems are the same

options.zh: 因为只看疼痛位置就够了
options.en: Because pain location alone is enough

options.zh: 因为不需要 vitals
options.en: Because vitals are unnecessary

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 35 不是在背病名，而是训练如何评估风险与严重度。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 35 is less about memorizing disease names and more about assessing risk and severity.

questions.questionZh: Toxicology 这一章为什么在 EMS 中很重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the toxicology chapter important in EMS?

options.zh: 因为中毒与药物滥用常以高风险急症形式出现
options.en: Because poisoning and substance misuse often present as high-risk emergencies

options.zh: 因为 toxicology 只属于实验室医学
options.en: Because toxicology belongs only to laboratory medicine

options.zh: 因为中毒病人都很容易识别
options.en: Because poisoned patients are always easy to identify

options.zh: 因为这章只适合医生看
options.en: Because this chapter is only useful for physicians

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 36 强调 substance abuse 与 poisoning 是院前高频且高风险主题。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 36 emphasizes that poisoning and substance misuse are common and high-risk prehospital topics.

questions.questionZh: 什么是 toxicology assessment 里很重要的一个能力？
questions.questionEn: What is an important skill in toxicology assessment?

options.zh: pattern recognition，例如识别 toxidrome
options.en: Pattern recognition, such as recognizing toxidromes

options.zh: 只靠病人自己诊断
options.en: Relying only on the patient to self-diagnose

options.zh: 跳过现场信息
options.en: Ignoring scene information

options.zh: 不做 reassessment
options.en: Avoiding reassessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 36 的一个核心点是通过模式识别帮助快速形成判断。
questions.explanationEn: A central idea in Chapter 36 is using pattern recognition to accelerate clinical reasoning.

questions.questionZh: Hematologic emergencies 这一章的价值更接近哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which best reflects the value of the hematologic emergencies chapter?

options.zh: 帮助理解血液系统问题及其院前处理原则
options.en: Helping learners understand blood-related emergencies and their prehospital management principles

options.zh: 只背血型
options.en: Memorizing blood types only

options.zh: 和急救无关
options.en: It has nothing to do with emergency care

options.zh: 只在手术室有用
options.en: It is only useful in the operating room

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 37 是把血液系统疾病和急症思维接起来。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 37 connects hematologic disease concepts with emergency management thinking.

questions.questionZh: 为什么不能忽略 hematologic condition 对整体病情的影响？
questions.questionEn: Why should the impact of hematologic conditions on the overall patient picture not be ignored?

options.zh: 因为它们可能影响灌注、携氧、出血与整体稳定性
options.en: Because they may affect perfusion, oxygen delivery, bleeding, and overall stability

options.zh: 因为只会影响检验数值
options.en: Because they only affect laboratory numbers

options.zh: 因为它们永远不紧急
options.en: Because they are never urgent

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不需要了解
options.en: Because paramedics do not need to understand them

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 37 的重要性在于把 blood-related problems 放回整体临床状态里看。
questions.explanationEn: The importance of Chapter 37 is understanding blood-related problems as part of the whole clinical picture.

questions.questionZh: Environmental emergencies 这章的核心是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core of the environmental emergencies chapter?

options.zh: 认识环境本身也可能是病因
options.en: Recognizing that the environment itself may be the cause of illness or injury

options.zh: 环境和病人状况没有关系
options.en: The environment has nothing to do with patient condition

options.zh: 只关注天气预报
options.en: Focusing only on the weather forecast

options.zh: 只适用于登山者
options.en: It only applies to mountain climbers

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 38 讲的是 heat, cold, drowning, altitude 等环境主导型急症。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 38 covers environment-driven emergencies such as heat, cold, drowning, and altitude illness.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 hypothermia / heat illness / drowning 应该放在同一大类理解？
questions.questionEn: Why should hypothermia, heat illness, and drowning be understood within the same broad category?

options.zh: 因为它们都显示环境暴露如何改变生理稳定性
options.en: Because they all show how environmental exposure can disrupt physiologic stability

options.zh: 因为它们都只影响皮肤
options.en: Because they only affect the skin

options.zh: 因为它们都不影响 ABC
options.en: Because they do not affect ABCs

options.zh: 因为它们只在夏天发生
options.en: Because they only occur in summer

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 38 的统一主线是：环境改变了病人的核心生理状态。
questions.explanationEn: The unifying thread of Chapter 38 is that the environment disrupts core physiology.

questions.questionZh: Infectious and communicable diseases 这一章为什么对 paramedic 重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the infectious and communicable diseases chapter important for paramedics?

options.zh: 因为它同时关系到病人照护、个人防护与公共卫生
options.en: Because it affects patient care, personal protection, and public health simultaneously

options.zh: 因为传染病只归医院处理
options.en: Because infectious disease is handled only by hospitals

options.zh: 因为这章只和实验室有关
options.en: Because the chapter only concerns laboratory science

options.zh: 因为救护车不需要清洁消毒
options.en: Because ambulances do not need cleaning or disinfection

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 39 把感染问题放在临床、职业安全和公共卫生交叉点上。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 39 places infectious disease at the intersection of clinical care, provider safety, and public health.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 ambulance cleaning and disinfection 被放进这一章？
questions.questionEn: Why is ambulance cleaning and disinfection included in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为感染控制不仅是理论，也直接影响现场与转运安全
options.en: Because infection control is not just theoretical; it directly affects operational and transport safety

options.zh: 因为只是 housekeeping 问题
options.en: Because it is merely a housekeeping issue

options.zh: 因为它和 communicable disease 无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to communicable disease

options.zh: 因为清洁只在班后做一次就够
options.en: Because cleaning once after shift is always enough

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 39 强调 infection control 必须落实到车辆和设备层面。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 39 emphasizes that infection control must extend to the ambulance and equipment environment.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 1 最核心的任务是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core purpose of Chapter 1?

options.zh: 教你只做病人搬运
options.en: To teach patient transport only

options.zh: 定义 EMS 系统、角色、责任与专业边界
options.en: To define EMS systems, roles, responsibilities, and professional boundaries

options.zh: 专门讲创伤止血
options.en: To focus specifically on trauma bleeding control

options.zh: 只讲 hospital workflow
options.en: To focus only on hospital workflow

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 是整本书的身份与系统入口，先定义你在 EMS 里是谁、做什么、承担什么责任。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 1 is the identity-and-system gateway to the book, defining who you are in EMS, what you do, and what responsibilities you hold.

questions.questionZh: EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities 这一章为什么应该先学？
questions.questionEn: Why should EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities be learned first?

options.zh: 因为它让后面所有 clinical action 都有系统与边界基础
options.en: Because it gives later clinical actions a system and boundary framework

options.zh: 因为它最短
options.en: Because it is the shortest chapter

options.zh: 因为它只考定义
options.en: Because it only tests definitions

options.zh: 因为它和后续章节没有关系
options.en: Because it has no relation to later chapters

questions.explanationZh: 如果 Chapter 1 不稳，后面很多 assessment、法律和决策内容都会失去框架。
questions.explanationEn: Without Chapter 1, many later ideas in assessment, law, and decision-making lose their framework.

questions.questionZh: The history of paramedicine 在这章里的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the significance of the history of paramedicine in this chapter?

options.zh: 只是背景故事，没有临床价值
options.en: It is only background with no professional value

options.zh: 帮助理解 EMS 为什么会发展成今天的系统与角色结构
options.en: It helps explain why EMS developed into today's system and role structure

options.zh: 只是为了记年份
options.en: It exists only for memorizing dates

options.zh: 只对研究人员重要
options.en: It matters only to researchers

questions.explanationZh: 历史让你理解当前 scope、training、medical direction 和 system design 的来由。
questions.explanationEn: The history explains the origins of current scope, training, medical direction, and system design.

questions.questionZh: 在 Chapter 1 的框架里，paramedic 最不应该被理解成什么？
questions.questionEn: Within Chapter 1, what should a paramedic least be understood as?

options.zh: 系统内的专业临床角色
options.en: A professional clinical role within a system

options.zh: 只负责把病人送到医院的人
options.en: Someone who only transports patients to hospital

options.zh: 需要与 medical direction 协同工作的人
options.en: Someone who works in coordination with medical direction

options.zh: 承担责任与记录义务的人
options.en: Someone with accountability and documentation duties

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 明确反对把 paramedic 简化成纯运输角色。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 1 explicitly rejects reducing the paramedic role to simple transport.

questions.questionZh: Licensure、certification、registration 在本章中共同服务于哪一类理解？
questions.questionEn: Licensure, certification, and registration in this chapter primarily support what kind of understanding?

options.zh: 职业资格、准入与责任框架
options.en: Professional qualification, entry, and accountability frameworks

options.zh: 病理生理机制
options.en: Pathophysiologic mechanisms

options.zh: 药物代谢
options.en: Drug metabolism

options.zh: 创伤分类
options.en: Trauma classification

questions.explanationZh: 这些概念是 Chapter 1 中职业身份与合法执业边界的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: These concepts belong to the chapter's framework for professional identity and lawful practice boundaries.

questions.questionZh: Professionalism 在 Chapter 1 中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is professionalism important in Chapter 1?

options.zh: 因为它只影响外表形象
options.en: Because it only affects outward appearance

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 的行为、沟通与责任感会直接影响病人、团队和系统信任
options.en: Because behavior, communication, and accountability directly affect patient, team, and system trust

options.zh: 因为只有管理层需要专业性
options.en: Because only managers need professionalism

options.zh: 因为它与病人安全无关
options.en: Because it has nothing to do with patient safety

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 把 professionalism 当作实际工作能力的一部分，而不是礼仪装饰。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 1 treats professionalism as part of real-world professional competence, not mere etiquette.

questions.questionZh: Roles and responsibilities 这一部分最核心的作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important function of the roles and responsibilities section?

options.zh: 让你知道在系统中该做什么、不能做什么、对谁负责
options.en: To clarify what you should do, should not do, and to whom you are accountable within the system

options.zh: 只为了写 job title
options.en: Only to provide a job title

options.zh: 只和薪资有关
options.en: It only relates to salary

options.zh: 与 clinical judgment 无关
options.en: It has nothing to do with clinical judgment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 的 roles/responsibilities 是后续 scope、法律、伦理和团队合作的基础。
questions.explanationEn: The roles/responsibilities section in Chapter 1 underpins later discussions of scope, law, ethics, and teamwork.

questions.questionZh: Medical direction 在 EMS 中的意义最准确的是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which best captures the meaning of medical direction in EMS?

options.zh: 它让现场照护和更大的医疗系统、协议与监督连接起来
options.en: It connects field care with the larger medical system, protocols, and oversight

options.zh: 它等于现场完全不能思考
options.en: It means no independent thinking is allowed in the field

options.zh: 它只在 paperwork 里出现
options.en: It appears only in paperwork

options.zh: 它与 patient care 没关系
options.en: It has nothing to do with patient care

questions.explanationZh: Medical direction 是 Chapter 1 的关键系统桥梁。
questions.explanationEn: Medical direction is a key system-level bridge in Chapter 1.

questions.questionZh: Improving system quality 这一部分为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the improving system quality section important?

options.zh: 因为 EMS 不该只维持现状，而要持续改进安全与结果
options.en: Because EMS should not merely maintain the status quo but continuously improve safety and outcomes

options.zh: 因为它只是追责工具
options.en: Because it is only a blame tool

options.zh: 因为只有医院需要 quality work
options.en: Because only hospitals need quality work

options.zh: 因为它只和统计表有关
options.en: Because it only concerns spreadsheets

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 里的 quality improvement 是成熟 EMS 系统的重要标志。
questions.explanationEn: Quality improvement in Chapter 1 is a hallmark of a mature EMS system.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 1 学完后，最应该建立的脑内模型是什么？
questions.questionEn: After Chapter 1, what internal model should be built first?

options.zh: EMS 是一个有角色、边界、责任、监督与质量框架的系统
options.en: EMS is a system with roles, boundaries, accountability, oversight, and quality structures

options.zh: EMS 只是现场技术动作的集合
options.en: EMS is merely a collection of field techniques

options.zh: paramedic 只需要会操作设备
options.en: A paramedic only needs to know how to operate equipment

options.zh: 系统知识不如单个技能重要
options.en: System knowledge matters less than isolated skills

questions.explanationZh: 这章的真正收获，是先建立系统观，而不是只背几个名词。
questions.explanationEn: The true gain from this chapter is a systems-based view, not just memorizing terms.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 2 最核心的主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 2?

options.zh: 只讲车祸创伤
options.en: It is only about motor vehicle trauma

options.zh: paramedic 的身心健康、自我保护与职业稳定性
options.en: Paramedic well-being, self-protection, and long-term professional sustainability

options.zh: 只讲 hospital 管理
options.en: It only covers hospital management

options.zh: 只讲药理学
options.en: It only covers pharmacology

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 讲的是急救人员如何在高压、高风险环境下保持安全、健康和可持续工作。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 focuses on how paramedics remain safe, healthy, and sustainable in high-stress, high-risk work environments.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 The Well-Being of the Paramedic 不是“软性附录”？
questions.questionEn: Why is The Well-Being of the Paramedic not just a soft add-on chapter?

options.zh: 因为它直接影响判断、现场安全和职业寿命
options.en: Because it directly affects judgment, scene safety, and career longevity

options.zh: 因为它比较短
options.en: Because it is relatively short

options.zh: 因为它只和情绪有关
options.en: Because it only concerns emotions

options.zh: 因为只有管理层需要看
options.en: Because only supervisors need to read it

questions.explanationZh: 这章的重点是：well-being 本身就是专业能力和安全的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter emphasizes that well-being itself is part of professional competence and safety.

questions.questionZh: Components of well-being 在本章中的作用最接近哪一项？
questions.questionEn: What is the role of the components of well-being in this chapter?

options.zh: 帮助理解 paramedic 健康不是单一维度问题
options.en: They show that paramedic well-being is not a single-dimension issue

options.zh: 只是理论分类，不影响实际工作
options.en: They are only theoretical categories with no practical impact

options.zh: 只适用于新手
options.en: They only apply to beginners

options.zh: 只和身体健康有关
options.en: They concern only physical health

questions.explanationZh: well-being 在这章中是多维的，包含心理、身体、职业压力与安全。
questions.explanationEn: Well-being in this chapter is multidimensional, including mental, physical, occupational, and safety dimensions.

questions.questionZh: Stress 为什么被放在 Chapter 2 的中心位置之一？
questions.questionEn: Why is stress one of the central topics in Chapter 2?

options.zh: 因为急救人员长期面对高压与不可预测事件
options.en: Because paramedics repeatedly face high-stress and unpredictable events

options.zh: 因为 stress 只影响个人心情，不影响工作
options.en: Because stress only affects mood and not work performance

options.zh: 因为 stress 可以完全忽略
options.en: Because stress can be completely ignored

options.zh: 因为 stress 只在夜班出现
options.en: Because stress exists only on night shifts

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 认为 stress 管理是职业现实，不是额外话题。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 treats stress management as an occupational reality, not an optional topic.

questions.questionZh: Coping with death and dying 这一部分为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the section on coping with death and dying important?

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 经常面对死亡、濒死与家属情绪冲击
options.en: Because paramedics frequently face death, dying, and intense family emotions

options.zh: 因为它只和 funeral service 有关
options.en: Because it only relates to funeral services

options.zh: 因为这只是心理学内容，与急救无关
options.en: Because it is merely psychology and unrelated to EMS

options.zh: 因为只有医生需要面对死亡
options.en: Because only physicians deal with death

questions.explanationZh: 这章提醒：面对死亡不是罕见例外，而是职业的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter reminds us that dealing with death is not a rare exception—it is part of the profession.

questions.questionZh: Emergency vehicle operations 为什么会出现在 well-being 这一章里？
questions.questionEn: Why do emergency vehicle operations appear in a chapter on well-being?

options.zh: 因为行车安全本身就是 paramedic 自我保护的一部分
options.en: Because driving safety is itself part of paramedic self-protection

options.zh: 因为这章其实在讲交通法规考试
options.en: Because the chapter is really a traffic law lesson

options.zh: 因为车辆和病人安全无关
options.en: Because vehicles have nothing to do with patient or provider safety

options.zh: 因为只有司机需要懂
options.en: Because only drivers need to understand it

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 把 emergency vehicle safety 视作职业风险管理的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 treats emergency vehicle safety as part of occupational risk management.

questions.questionZh: Protecting yourself 在本章中最重要的含义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important meaning of protecting yourself in this chapter?

options.zh: 把自我保护当成职业责任，而不是自私行为
options.en: Viewing self-protection as a professional responsibility rather than selfishness

options.zh: 只在极端灾害时才需要保护自己
options.en: Only protecting yourself during extreme disasters

options.zh: 把病人永远放在自己安全之前
options.en: Always placing the patient before personal safety

options.zh: 避免和任何病人接触
options.en: Avoiding contact with all patients

questions.explanationZh: 本章最重要的底层原则之一就是：先保护自己，才能继续安全照护病人。
questions.explanationEn: One core principle of the chapter is that provider safety comes first so patient care can continue safely.

questions.questionZh: 关于 burnout / chronic stress，Chapter 2 最合理的态度是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most appropriate attitude toward burnout and chronic stress in Chapter 2?

options.zh: 硬撑到底，不要处理
options.en: Push through and do nothing about it

options.zh: 把它们视为可能影响职业表现与健康的真实风险
options.en: Treat them as real risks that may affect professional performance and health

options.zh: 只要经验多了就自动消失
options.en: They automatically disappear with experience

options.zh: 只会发生在能力差的人身上
options.en: They only happen to weak providers

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 的态度是识别、管理、预防，而不是否认。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 promotes recognition, management, and prevention rather than denial.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Chapter 2 会影响后面的 clinical performance？
questions.questionEn: Why does Chapter 2 affect later clinical performance?

options.zh: 因为 provider 的状态会影响判断、沟通、驾驶、安全与执行质量
options.en: Because provider condition affects judgment, communication, driving, safety, and execution quality

options.zh: 因为它只是背景阅读，不会影响实战
options.en: Because it is only background reading with no practical effect

options.zh: 因为它只和 paper assignment 有关
options.en: Because it only matters for written assignments

options.zh: 因为它只影响休假安排
options.en: Because it only affects vacation scheduling

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 的价值在于，它直接关系到你能不能稳定、清醒、安全地工作。
questions.explanationEn: The value of Chapter 2 lies in its direct link to stable, clear, and safe professional performance.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 2 学完后最该建立的一个职业观是什么？
questions.questionEn: What professional mindset should be built after Chapter 2?

options.zh: 照护别人和保护自己是同一套专业责任的一部分
options.en: Caring for others and protecting yourself are part of the same professional responsibility

options.zh: 自我保护和专业精神是冲突的
options.en: Self-protection conflicts with professionalism

options.zh: 只要技术好，压力管理不重要
options.en: If your technical skills are strong, stress management does not matter

options.zh: 急救人员不该讨论自己的状态
options.en: Paramedics should never discuss their own condition

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 最重要的职业观之一，就是自我保护不是软弱，而是专业。
questions.explanationEn: One of the chapter’s key professional messages is that self-protection is not weakness—it is professionalism.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 3 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 3?

options.zh: 疾病与伤害预防
options.en: Illness and injury prevention

options.zh: 只讲 hospital discharge
options.en: Hospital discharge only

options.zh: 只讲手术器械
options.en: Surgical instruments only

options.zh: 只讲药物计算
options.en: Medication calculation only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 3 把 EMS 的角色从事后反应，延伸到前端 prevention。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 3 extends EMS from reactive response into prevention work.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Injury Prevention and EMS 会放在同一章？
questions.questionEn: Why are injury prevention and EMS placed together in one chapter?

options.zh: 因为 EMS 不只是出事后处理，也可以参与减少未来事件发生
options.en: Because EMS not only responds after events occur, but can also help reduce future events

options.zh: 因为 EMS 只做社区宣传
options.en: Because EMS only does community education

options.zh: 因为 prevention 与 paramedic 无关
options.en: Because prevention is unrelated to paramedics

options.zh: 因为这一章只是理论补充
options.en: Because this chapter is only theoretical filler

questions.explanationZh: 这章的重点是让你看到 EMS 也有 public health / prevention 面向。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter highlights the public health and prevention role of EMS.

questions.questionZh: Principles of injury prevention 在这一章中的作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the role of the principles of injury prevention in this chapter?

options.zh: 帮助把“少发生事故”变成可执行的系统思路
options.en: They help turn 'fewer injuries' into an actionable systems-based approach

options.zh: 只为了记定义
options.en: They exist only for memorizing definitions

options.zh: 只适用于儿童学校教育
options.en: They only apply to school-based child education

options.zh: 和 EMS 现场工作无关
options.en: They are unrelated to field EMS work

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 3 不是喊口号，而是在讲 prevention 要怎么系统化地开展。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 3 is not about slogans; it is about how prevention can be approached systematically.

questions.questionZh: Getting started in your community 这一部分最想训练什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main purpose of the 'Getting started in your community' section?

options.zh: 让 paramedic 理解如何把 prevention 落到真实社区环境里
options.en: To help paramedics understand how to implement prevention in real community settings

options.zh: 让 paramedic 离开临床岗位
options.en: To move paramedics away from clinical roles

options.zh: 只做行政表格
options.en: To focus only on administrative forms

options.zh: 只适用于大城市系统
options.en: It only applies to large urban systems

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 3 试图让 prevention 变成现实可做的工作，而不是抽象概念。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 3 tries to make prevention practical rather than abstract.

questions.questionZh: How Every Paramedic Can Be Involved 这一段的核心信息是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the key message of 'How Every Paramedic Can Be Involved'?

options.zh: 不是只有管理层或公共卫生部门才能参与 prevention
options.en: Prevention is not limited to managers or public health departments

options.zh: 只有高级 paramedic 才能做 prevention
options.en: Only advanced paramedics can engage in prevention

options.zh: 临床人员不应该参与 prevention
options.en: Clinical personnel should not be involved in prevention

options.zh: prevention 与 EMS 价值冲突
options.en: Prevention conflicts with EMS values

questions.explanationZh: 这章在扩大 paramedic 的职业理解：每个人都可以在 prevention 上有角色。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter broadens the professional role of paramedics by showing that everyone can contribute to prevention.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 3 为什么会影响你看待 EMS 的方式？
questions.questionEn: Why does Chapter 3 change the way you view EMS?

options.zh: 因为它让 EMS 从“反应型系统”变成也具有“前端预防价值”的系统
options.en: Because it expands EMS from a reactive system into one with preventive value

options.zh: 因为它让 EMS 只做教育不救人
options.en: Because it turns EMS into education only

options.zh: 因为它否定 emergency response 的价值
options.en: Because it denies the value of emergency response

options.zh: 因为它只讨论统计学
options.en: Because it only discusses statistics

questions.explanationZh: 这是本章最重要的观念转变。
questions.explanationEn: This is one of the most important perspective shifts in the chapter.

questions.questionZh: 在这一章里，community 的意义更接近哪一个？
questions.questionEn: In this chapter, what does 'community' most closely represent?

options.zh: 真实发生伤病风险与 prevention 介入的环境
options.en: The real environment where injury risk exists and prevention interventions can occur

options.zh: 只指 hospital waiting room
options.en: Only the hospital waiting room

options.zh: 只指 online forum
options.en: Only online forums

options.zh: 与 EMS 无关的外部区域
options.en: An external area unrelated to EMS

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 3 的 community 是 prevention 的现实场景。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 3, the community is the real setting where prevention work happens.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 3 最不希望你留下的误解是什么？
questions.questionEn: What misunderstanding does Chapter 3 most want to prevent?

options.zh: EMS 只能在事故发生后才有价值
options.en: That EMS only has value after an incident has already happened

options.zh: prevention 需要系统化思考
options.en: That prevention requires systems thinking

options.zh: paramedic 也能参与社区层面的改善
options.en: That paramedics can help with community-level improvement

options.zh: 伤病预防是 EMS 视野的一部分
options.en: That prevention belongs within the EMS perspective

questions.explanationZh: 本章最重要的突破之一，就是反对把 EMS 只看成“收残局”的系统。
questions.explanationEn: One of the chapter's most important messages is that EMS is not just a cleanup system after harm occurs.

questions.questionZh: 如果把 Chapter 3 用一句话概括，最准确的是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: If Chapter 3 were summarized in one sentence, which option is best?

options.zh: EMS 不只是救后果，也可以参与减少后果发生
options.en: EMS not only treats consequences, but can also help reduce their occurrence

options.zh: EMS 主要任务是写 prevention 报告
options.en: The main job of EMS is writing prevention reports

options.zh: EMS 应该退出临床转做公共卫生
options.en: EMS should leave clinical care and become public health only

options.zh: prevention 比 emergency care 更重要
options.en: Prevention is more important than emergency care

questions.explanationZh: 这句话最接近本章的灵魂。
questions.explanationEn: This sentence best captures the spirit of the chapter.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 3 学完后，你应该建立哪种职业延伸观？
questions.questionEn: What expanded professional mindset should you build after Chapter 3?

options.zh: paramedic 不只是在 emergency 中反应，也能在社区层面帮助降低风险
options.en: A paramedic not only reacts in emergencies but can also help reduce risk at the community level

options.zh: paramedic 不需要理解 prevention
options.en: A paramedic does not need to understand prevention

options.zh: prevention 只和政府部门有关
options.en: Prevention only concerns government agencies

options.zh: EMS 与 public health 互不相干
options.en: EMS and public health are unrelated

questions.explanationZh: 这是 Chapter 3 最值得留下来的职业视野。
questions.explanationEn: This is the professional perspective most worth retaining from Chapter 3.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 4 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 4?

options.zh: 医疗与法律议题
options.en: Medical and legal issues

options.zh: 只讲药物剂量
options.en: Medication doses only

options.zh: 只讲儿童评估
options.en: Pediatric assessment only

options.zh: 只讲创伤转运
options.en: Trauma transport only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 是整本书里最关键的法律边界章之一。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 is one of the book’s key chapters for legal and professional boundaries.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 The Legal System in Canada 会放在这章里？
questions.questionEn: Why is The Legal System in Canada included in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 的实践必须放在法律框架中理解
options.en: Because paramedic practice must be understood within a legal framework

options.zh: 因为这只是背景阅读
options.en: Because it is only background reading

options.zh: 因为只有律师需要懂
options.en: Because only lawyers need to know it

options.zh: 因为 clinical work 不涉及法律
options.en: Because clinical work does not involve legal issues

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 要你理解：你做的每件事都不是脱离法律环境的。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 teaches that none of your actions occur outside a legal context.

questions.questionZh: Legal accountability of the paramedic 这部分最核心在讲什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main point of legal accountability of the paramedic?

options.zh: paramedic 同样要对自己的专业行为负责
options.en: Paramedics are also accountable for their professional actions

options.zh: 只有医生承担责任
options.en: Only physicians bear accountability

options.zh: 只要出于好意就不需要负责
options.en: Good intentions remove accountability

options.zh: 法律责任只和 hospital 有关
options.en: Legal accountability only exists in hospitals

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 明确：paramedic 不是系统中的免责角色。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 makes it clear that paramedics are not exempt from accountability within the system.

questions.questionZh: Negligence 在这章里为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is negligence important in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为它帮助理解 duty、标准、偏差与伤害之间的关系
options.en: Because it helps explain the relationship among duty, standard of care, breach, and harm

options.zh: 因为它只是一个法学院术语
options.en: Because it is only a law-school term

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不会涉及 negligence
options.en: Because paramedics never face negligence issues

options.zh: 因为只和保险理赔有关
options.en: Because it only concerns insurance claims

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 里 negligence 不是抽象概念，而是实际职业风险。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 4, negligence is not abstract; it is a real professional risk.

questions.questionZh: Protection against negligence claims 这一部分最希望你建立什么意识？
questions.questionEn: What awareness does the section on protection against negligence claims aim to build?

options.zh: 规范实践、合理记录、遵循标准本身就是保护的一部分
options.en: Sound practice, proper documentation, and adherence to standards are themselves part of protection

options.zh: 最好的方式是不写记录
options.en: The best protection is to avoid documentation

options.zh: 只要病人喜欢你就没问题
options.en: As long as the patient likes you, there is no risk

options.zh: 只有出事后才需要考虑法律保护
options.en: Legal protection only matters after something goes wrong

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 的保护思路不是躲，而是规范地做。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 frames protection not as avoidance but as consistently practicing properly.

questions.questionZh: Paramedic–patient relationship 在法律上为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the paramedic–patient relationship legally important?

options.zh: 因为它涉及责任、信任、义务和专业边界
options.en: Because it involves duty, trust, obligations, and professional boundaries

options.zh: 因为只是礼貌问题
options.en: Because it is just about politeness

options.zh: 因为关系只影响 hospital 满意度
options.en: Because it only affects hospital satisfaction

options.zh: 因为它与 legal issues 无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to legal issues

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 把 paramedic–patient relationship 放在责任与边界框架里。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 places the paramedic–patient relationship within a framework of duty and boundaries.

questions.questionZh: Crime scene and emergency scene responsibilities 为什么会放进这章？
questions.questionEn: Why are crime scene and emergency scene responsibilities included in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为现场行为也有法律与专业后果
options.en: Because actions on scene carry legal and professional consequences

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 主要任务是调查犯罪
options.en: Because the main role of paramedics is criminal investigation

options.zh: 因为犯罪现场和 EMS 无关
options.en: Because crime scenes are unrelated to EMS

options.zh: 因为这只适用于警察系统
options.en: Because it applies only to police systems

questions.explanationZh: 这部分提醒你：你在现场的行为不仅是临床行为，也是可被追溯和评价的专业行为。
questions.explanationEn: This section reminds you that scene actions are not only clinical acts but also professionally accountable ones.

questions.questionZh: Documentation 在 Chapter 4 中最不应该被理解成什么？
questions.questionEn: What should documentation in Chapter 4 never be reduced to?

options.zh: 烦人的行政工作而已
options.en: Merely annoying administrative work

options.zh: 临床与法律双重重要的记录
options.en: A clinically and legally important record

options.zh: 连续照护的一部分
options.en: Part of continuity of care

options.zh: 专业责任的体现
options.en: An expression of professional responsibility

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 里 documentation 绝不是可有可无的附属动作。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 4, documentation is never an optional or trivial activity.

questions.questionZh: Reportable cases 这一部分让你最该记住什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important lesson from the reportable cases section?

options.zh: 有些情况不是个人想报不报，而是有制度性的上报责任
options.en: Some situations carry formal reporting obligations rather than personal discretion

options.zh: 只有死亡案件需要报告
options.en: Only deaths must be reported

options.zh: 所有病例都必须报给所有机构
options.en: Every case must be reported to every authority

options.zh: reportable cases 只对医生适用
options.en: Reportable cases apply only to physicians

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 的重点是：有些 case 有明确的法定或制度性责任。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 teaches that some cases trigger explicit legal or policy-based reporting obligations.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 4 学完后最该形成的底层观念是什么？
questions.questionEn: What foundational mindset should be formed after Chapter 4?

options.zh: paramedic 的每个临床动作都同时有法律和专业维度
options.en: Every clinical act by a paramedic also has legal and professional dimensions

options.zh: 只要技术对了，法律就不重要
options.en: If the technique is correct, law does not matter

options.zh: 法律问题只在法庭才出现
options.en: Legal issues only exist in courtrooms

options.zh: EMS 是法律之外的紧急例外区
options.en: EMS exists outside normal legal boundaries because it is emergency work

questions.explanationZh: 这是 Chapter 4 最核心的职业认识。
questions.explanationEn: This is the central professional insight of Chapter 4.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 5 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 5?

options.zh: 伦理议题
options.en: Ethical issues

options.zh: 只讲 ECG
options.en: ECG only

options.zh: 只讲车辆操作
options.en: Vehicle operations only

options.zh: 只讲烧伤
options.en: Burns only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 5 是伦理判断的核心章节。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 5 is a core chapter on ethical reasoning.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Ethical Issues 不能被当成“有空再看”的章节？
questions.questionEn: Why should Ethical Issues not be treated as an optional chapter?

options.zh: 因为临床中很多决定不只是能不能做，还牵涉应不应该做
options.en: Because many clinical decisions concern not only what can be done but what ought to be done

options.zh: 因为它只影响医院管理
options.en: Because it only affects hospital management

options.zh: 因为它只用于论文
options.en: Because it is only for essays

options.zh: 因为它和病人无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 5 处理的是临床灰区中的价值判断。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 5 deals with value judgments in clinical gray zones.

questions.questionZh: Patient autonomy 在本章中的核心含义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core meaning of patient autonomy in this chapter?

options.zh: 病人在有能力决策时，其选择应被认真看待
options.en: When the patient has decision-making capacity, their choices must be taken seriously

options.zh: 病人总是医学上最正确的
options.en: The patient is always medically correct

options.zh: 自主只存在于签字时
options.en: Autonomy exists only during signing forms

options.zh: 自主只适用于轻症
options.en: Autonomy only applies to minor illness

questions.explanationZh: 本章强调 autonomy 是伦理核心之一。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter emphasizes autonomy as a central ethical principle.

questions.questionZh: Withholding or withdrawing resuscitation 这一部分为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the section on withholding or withdrawing resuscitation important?

options.zh: 因为 resuscitation 决策常涉及伦理、法律与病人价值
options.en: Because resuscitation decisions often involve ethics, law, and patient values

options.zh: 因为所有病人都不该复苏
options.en: Because no patient should ever be resuscitated

options.zh: 因为这和 EMS 无关
options.en: Because this is unrelated to EMS

options.zh: 因为它只属于 ICU
options.en: Because it belongs only to the ICU

questions.explanationZh: 这是本章里非常现实的伦理难题。
questions.explanationEn: This is one of the chapter’s most practical ethical challenges.

questions.questionZh: Professional integrity and roles 这一部分最提醒什么？
questions.questionEn: What does the section on professional integrity and roles mainly remind us of?

options.zh: 专业角色之间可能出现冲突，需要有伦理判断
options.en: Professional roles may conflict and require ethical judgment

options.zh: 只要规则写了就永远不会有冲突
options.en: If rules are written, conflict never occurs

options.zh: 诚信只和个人形象有关
options.en: Integrity is only about image

options.zh: 它与 patient care 无关
options.en: It is unrelated to patient care

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 5 强调真实工作里会出现角色冲突与价值冲突。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 5 emphasizes real-world role and value conflicts.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Organ Donation 会被放进 Ethical Issues？
questions.questionEn: Why is organ donation included in Ethical Issues?

options.zh: 因为它涉及病人意愿、家庭、时机与伦理框架
options.en: Because it involves patient wishes, family, timing, and ethical frameworks

options.zh: 因为它只是技术流程
options.en: Because it is only a technical process

options.zh: 因为它和病人自主无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to autonomy

options.zh: 因为只有外科需要懂
options.en: Because only surgeons need to understand it

questions.explanationZh: Organ donation 在本章中属于典型伦理议题。
questions.explanationEn: Organ donation is a classic ethical topic within this chapter.

questions.questionZh: Ethics in EMS research 为什么会出现在这章？
questions.questionEn: Why does ethics in EMS research appear in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为研究也必须受伦理框架约束
options.en: Because research must also be governed by ethical frameworks

options.zh: 因为研究不需要伦理
options.en: Because research requires no ethics

options.zh: 因为只有大学才做研究
options.en: Because only universities conduct research

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不会参与研究
options.en: Because paramedics never participate in research

questions.explanationZh: 本章强调伦理不仅存在于 bedside decision，也存在于 research。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter emphasizes that ethics applies not only to bedside decisions but also to research.

questions.questionZh: Ethics in the EMS workplace: accountability 最想强调什么？
questions.questionEn: What does ethics in the EMS workplace: accountability most emphasize?

options.zh: 工作场域中的伦理也包含责任与可问责性
options.en: Ethics in the workplace also includes responsibility and accountability

options.zh: 伦理只在对病人时才重要
options.en: Ethics matters only when directly facing the patient

options.zh: accountability 只属于法律章
options.en: Accountability belongs only in the legal chapter

options.zh: 只要技术好就不需要伦理
options.en: If technical skill is strong, ethics is unnecessary

questions.explanationZh: 伦理不是脱离工作环境的抽象概念。
questions.explanationEn: Ethics is not an abstract concept detached from the workplace.

questions.questionZh: 学完 Chapter 5 后，最该留下的观念是什么？
questions.questionEn: What key idea should remain after studying Chapter 5?

options.zh: 好的临床不只靠技术，也靠伦理判断
options.en: Good clinical care depends not only on technical skill but also on ethical judgment

options.zh: 伦理只会拖慢救人
options.en: Ethics only slows down care

options.zh: 伦理和法律完全一样
options.en: Ethics and law are exactly the same

options.zh: 伦理只是个人喜好
options.en: Ethics is merely personal preference

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 5 的真正价值是建立灰区判断能力。
questions.explanationEn: The true value of Chapter 5 is building judgment in gray zones.

questions.questionZh: 如果用一句话概括 Chapter 5，最准确的是？
questions.questionEn: If Chapter 5 were summarized in one sentence, which is best?

options.zh: 临床上不只是能不能做，还要想应不应该做
options.en: In clinical care, the question is not only what can be done, but what ought to be done

options.zh: 只要能做就应该做
options.en: If something can be done, it should be done

options.zh: 所有伦理问题都有标准答案
options.en: All ethical problems have one standard answer

options.zh: 伦理只会出现在教室里
options.en: Ethics exists only in the classroom

questions.explanationZh: 这句话最接近 Chapter 5 的灵魂。
questions.explanationEn: This sentence best captures the spirit of Chapter 5.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 6 最核心的主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 6?

options.zh: 病理生理学
options.en: Pathophysiology

options.zh: 只讲药名
options.en: Drug names only

options.zh: 只讲儿童成长
options.en: Child development only

options.zh: 只讲交通规则
options.en: Traffic rules only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 是理解疾病机制的基础章。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 6 is a foundational chapter for understanding disease mechanisms.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Pathophysiology 是后面很多章节的底座？
questions.questionEn: Why is pathophysiology the foundation for many later chapters?

options.zh: 因为它解释病人为什么会变成现在这样
options.en: Because it explains why the patient has become this way

options.zh: 因为它只适合考试背诵
options.en: Because it is only useful for exam memorization

options.zh: 因为它和 clinical decision 无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to clinical decisions

options.zh: 因为它只对医生重要
options.en: Because it is important only to physicians

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 提供 disease process 的底层理解。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 6 provides the underlying understanding of disease processes.

questions.questionZh: Acid–base balance 为什么会放进这一章？
questions.questionEn: Why is acid-base balance included in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为很多急症会涉及生理平衡失调
options.en: Because many emergencies involve physiologic imbalance

options.zh: 因为它只是化学课内容
options.en: Because it is only chemistry-class content

options.zh: 因为 EMS 不需要理解生理平衡
options.en: Because EMS does not need to understand physiologic balance

options.zh: 因为它只影响实验室报告
options.en: Because it only affects lab reports

questions.explanationZh: 酸碱平衡是 Chapter 6 中重要的生理基础。
questions.explanationEn: Acid-base balance is an important physiologic foundation in Chapter 6.

questions.questionZh: Hypoperfusion 在 Chapter 6 中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is hypoperfusion important in Chapter 6?

options.zh: 因为它连接了灌注不足与器官/细胞损伤
options.en: Because it links inadequate perfusion with organ and cellular injury

options.zh: 因为它只和创伤有关
options.en: Because it only relates to trauma

options.zh: 因为它不影响病人结局
options.en: Because it does not affect outcomes

options.zh: 因为它只是一个术语
options.en: Because it is just a term

questions.explanationZh: Hypoperfusion 是很多危重病过程的核心概念。
questions.explanationEn: Hypoperfusion is a central concept in many critical illness processes.

questions.questionZh: The body’s self-defence mechanisms 这一部分在教什么？
questions.questionEn: What does the body’s self-defence mechanisms section teach?

options.zh: 人体有维持与对抗损伤/感染的内在反应系统
options.en: The body has intrinsic systems for maintaining balance and responding to injury/infection

options.zh: 人体对疾病没有主动反应
options.en: The body has no active response to disease

options.zh: 这部分只讲外伤包扎
options.en: It only covers wound dressing

options.zh: 这部分和免疫无关
options.en: It is unrelated to immunity

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 帮你理解人体不是被动受害者，而有自我调节与防御机制。
