questions.explanationEn: Chapter 6 helps you understand that the body is not passive; it has regulatory and defensive mechanisms.

questions.questionZh: Inflammation 在这章里为什么关键？
questions.questionEn: Why is inflammation a key concept in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为它是很多急症和疾病过程里的共同主线之一
options.en: Because it is one of the common threads across many disease and emergency processes

options.zh: 因为它只会发生在感染
options.en: Because it occurs only in infection

options.zh: 因为它永远是坏事
options.en: Because it is always harmful

options.zh: 因为它只在慢性病里出现
options.en: Because it appears only in chronic disease

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 让你看到 inflammation 是广泛存在的病理过程。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 6 shows that inflammation is a broad and recurring pathophysiologic process.

questions.questionZh: Genetics and familial disease 为什么也会被放进 Pathophysiology？
questions.questionEn: Why are genetics and familial disease included in Pathophysiology?

options.zh: 因为疾病机制不只来自外界，也可能与遗传背景有关
options.en: Because disease mechanisms arise not only from external factors but also from genetic background

options.zh: 因为遗传和急救无关
options.en: Because genetics are unrelated to emergency care

options.zh: 因为这只用于科研人员
options.en: Because this matters only to researchers

options.zh: 因为 familial disease 只是统计学概念
options.en: Because familial disease is only a statistical concept

questions.explanationZh: 本章把疾病来源看得更完整，不只盯着当下症状。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter broadens disease understanding beyond immediate symptoms alone.

questions.questionZh: Stress and disease 为什么会出现在这一章末尾？
questions.questionEn: Why does stress and disease appear near the end of this chapter?

options.zh: 因为压力本身也能影响病理过程和健康状态
options.en: Because stress itself can influence pathophysiology and health status

options.zh: 因为压力只影响心情，不影响身体
options.en: Because stress affects mood but not the body

options.zh: 因为这只是补充闲聊
options.en: Because it is just casual filler

options.zh: 因为压力和 disease process 无关
options.en: Because stress is unrelated to disease processes

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 把 stress 也纳入生理与病理的整体框架。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 6 includes stress within the broader physiologic and pathophysiologic framework.

questions.questionZh: 学完 Chapter 6 最该形成什么底层能力？
questions.questionEn: What foundational ability should be formed after Chapter 6?

options.zh: 能从机制角度理解病人为何出现这些体征和症状
options.en: To understand why patients show certain signs and symptoms from a mechanism-based perspective

options.zh: 只会背术语定义
options.en: To memorize terminology only

options.zh: 忽略 physiology 直接下结论
options.en: To ignore physiology and jump to conclusions

options.zh: 把所有病都看成彼此无关
options.en: To treat all disease processes as unrelated

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 6 的真正作用，是让你开始用“为什么”去理解病人。
questions.explanationEn: The true role of Chapter 6 is to train you to ask why a patient looks the way they do.

questions.questionZh: 如果用一句话概括 Chapter 6，最准确的是？
questions.questionEn: If Chapter 6 were summarized in one sentence, which is best?

options.zh: 它在解释疾病和危重状态背后的生理与病理机制
options.en: It explains the physiologic and pathophysiologic mechanisms behind disease and critical illness

options.zh: 它主要是在背 anatomy 名词
options.en: It is mainly about memorizing anatomy terms

options.zh: 它只适合 advanced specialist
options.en: It is only for advanced specialists

options.zh: 它与院前急救无关
options.en: It is unrelated to prehospital care

questions.explanationZh: 这句话最接近 Pathophysiology 章节的核心价值。
questions.explanationEn: This sentence best captures the core value of the pathophysiology chapter.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 7 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 7?

options.zh: 药理学
options.en: Pharmacology

options.zh: 只讲创伤评估
options.en: Trauma assessment only

options.zh: 只讲分娩
options.en: Obstetrics only

options.zh: 只讲行为急症
options.en: Behavioral emergencies only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 7 是整本书里药理学基础章节。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 7 is the foundational pharmacology chapter in the book.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Pharmacology 是后面大量章节的基础？
questions.questionEn: Why is pharmacology foundational to many later chapters?

options.zh: 因为药物选择、作用、风险与给药都离不开它
options.en: Because medication choice, action, risk, and administration all depend on it

options.zh: 因为它只用于背药名
options.en: Because it is only for memorizing names

options.zh: 因为只有药剂师需要懂
options.en: Because only pharmacists need it

options.zh: 因为院前急救不用药
options.en: Because prehospital care does not use medications

questions.explanationZh: 这章是后续 medication decision-making 的底层框架。
questions.explanationEn: This chapter forms the underlying framework for later medication decision-making.

questions.questionZh: Medication names 这一部分的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the value of the medication names section?

options.zh: 帮助你识别药物的不同命名方式，避免沟通混乱
options.en: It helps you recognize different naming systems and avoid communication confusion

options.zh: 只是为了拼字考试
options.en: It exists only for spelling tests

options.zh: 药名不重要，只看颜色
options.en: Drug names do not matter; only color matters

options.zh: 只对药房有用
options.en: It is only useful in pharmacies

questions.explanationZh: 药名系统影响沟通、记录与用药安全。
questions.explanationEn: Medication naming systems affect communication, documentation, and medication safety.

questions.questionZh: Sources of drug information 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are sources of drug information important?

options.zh: 因为安全用药依赖可靠信息来源
options.en: Because safe medication use depends on reliable information sources

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 可以随便猜药效
options.en: Because paramedics can guess drug effects freely

options.zh: 因为只要记住一个药就行
options.en: Because knowing one drug is enough

options.zh: 因为用药不需要查证
options.en: Because medication use needs no verification

questions.explanationZh: 药理学里可靠信息来源是 patient safety 的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: Reliable information sources are part of patient safety in pharmacology.

questions.questionZh: Canadian regulation of pharmaceuticals 放进这一章的原因是什么？
questions.questionEn: Why is Canadian regulation of pharmaceuticals included in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为药物的使用和获取受制度与监管框架影响
options.en: Because medication use and access are shaped by regulatory systems

options.zh: 因为监管和临床完全无关
options.en: Because regulation is unrelated to clinical practice

options.zh: 因为这只是法律细节
options.en: Because it is only legal trivia

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不会遇到受监管药物
options.en: Because paramedics never encounter regulated drugs

questions.explanationZh: 药物不是脱离系统存在的，Chapter 7 把药理放回真实制度环境。
questions.explanationEn: Medications do not exist outside systems; Chapter 7 places pharmacology back into its real regulatory environment.

questions.questionZh: Routes of medication administration 为什么是高价值内容？
questions.questionEn: Why are routes of medication administration high-value content?

options.zh: 因为给药途径会影响吸收速度、效果和适用场景
options.en: Because the route affects absorption speed, effect, and clinical appropriateness

options.zh: 因为途径都一样
options.en: Because all routes are the same

options.zh: 因为 route 只对护士重要
options.en: Because routes matter only to nurses

options.zh: 因为 route 不影响药效
options.en: Because route does not affect drug effect

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 7 里 route 是药效与临床决策的重要部分。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 7, route is a major part of both pharmacology and clinical decision-making.

questions.questionZh: Pharmacokinetics 在这章里主要关心什么？
questions.questionEn: What does pharmacokinetics mainly concern in this chapter?

options.zh: 药物如何进入、分布、代谢与排出
options.en: How the drug enters, distributes, is metabolized, and is eliminated

options.zh: 药物道德问题
options.en: Drug ethics

options.zh: 药物包装颜色
options.en: Packaging color

options.zh: 只关心药价
options.en: Only drug price

questions.explanationZh: Pharmacokinetics 解释药物在体内“发生了什么”。
questions.explanationEn: Pharmacokinetics explains what happens to a drug in the body.

questions.questionZh: Pharmacodynamics 在这章里最重要的含义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important meaning of pharmacodynamics in this chapter?

options.zh: 药物对身体产生什么作用
options.en: What the drug does to the body

options.zh: 身体如何排出药物
options.en: How the body eliminates the drug

options.zh: 药物如何包装
options.en: How the drug is packaged

options.zh: 药物如何命名
options.en: How the drug is named

questions.explanationZh: Pharmacodynamics 解释“药对人做了什么”。
questions.explanationEn: Pharmacodynamics explains what the drug does to the person.

questions.questionZh: Medication forms 为什么值得单独学习？
questions.questionEn: Why are medication forms worth studying separately?

options.zh: 因为不同剂型会影响给药方式、吸收和使用场景
options.en: Because different dosage forms affect administration, absorption, and use scenarios

options.zh: 因为形式只影响外观
options.en: Because form only affects appearance

options.zh: 因为液体和片剂完全一样
options.en: Because liquids and tablets are exactly the same

options.zh: 因为剂型不影响临床
options.en: Because dosage form has no clinical impact

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 7 强调 medication form 不是外观问题，而是 clinical issue。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 7 emphasizes that dosage form is a clinical issue, not just a visual one.

questions.questionZh: Medication storage 为什么会被列入药理基础？
questions.questionEn: Why is medication storage included as part of basic pharmacology?

options.zh: 因为储存条件会影响药物稳定性和安全性
options.en: Because storage conditions affect medication stability and safety

options.zh: 因为只和仓库管理员有关
options.en: Because it only concerns warehouse staff

options.zh: 因为储存不影响药效
options.en: Because storage does not affect drug action

options.zh: 因为所有药都一样耐放
options.en: Because all drugs tolerate storage equally

questions.explanationZh: 不正确储存本身就可能成为 patient safety 问题。
questions.explanationEn: Improper storage itself can become a patient safety issue.

questions.questionZh: Components of a medication profile 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are components of a medication profile important?

options.zh: 因为了解药物相关关键信息有助于安全使用与判断
options.en: Because knowing key medication information supports safe use and sound judgment

options.zh: 因为 profile 只是药房记录
options.en: Because a profile is only a pharmacy record

options.zh: 因为名字对了就够了
options.en: Because the name alone is enough

options.zh: 因为只在 hospital 用得到
options.en: Because it is only useful in hospitals

questions.explanationZh: Medication profile 帮助把药物信息变成可用临床判断。
questions.explanationEn: A medication profile helps convert drug information into usable clinical judgment.

questions.questionZh: Medication classifications 为什么不能只死背？
questions.questionEn: Why should medication classifications not be memorized in a purely rote way?

options.zh: 因为分类真正帮助你理解药物作用群组和使用逻辑
options.en: Because classification helps you understand action groups and clinical logic

options.zh: 因为分类完全没用
options.en: Because classifications are useless

options.zh: 因为只记商品名就行
options.en: Because knowing brand names is enough

options.zh: 因为分类只给考试看
options.en: Because classifications are only for exams

questions.explanationZh: 分类的价值在于组织临床思维，而不只是背表格。
questions.explanationEn: The value of classification is organizing clinical reasoning, not memorizing tables.

questions.questionZh: Special considerations in medication therapy 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are special considerations in medication therapy important?

options.zh: 因为病人情况、环境与风险会改变用药决策
options.en: Because patient condition, context, and risk can alter medication decisions

options.zh: 因为所有病人用药都一样
options.en: Because all patients are medicated the same way

options.zh: 因为用药没有例外情况
options.en: Because there are no exceptions in medication use

options.zh: 因为这部分只适用于慢性病
options.en: Because it only applies to chronic disease

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 7 强调 medication decision 需要 context-sensitive thinking。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 7 emphasizes that medication decisions must be context-sensitive.

questions.questionZh: The scope of management 在本章中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the scope of management important in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为药物管理和使用必须放在合法执业范围内理解
options.en: Because medication management and use must be understood within scope of practice

options.zh: 因为 scope 和 pharmacology 无关
options.en: Because scope is unrelated to pharmacology

options.zh: 因为只要会用药就不用管 scope
options.en: Because once you know a drug, scope no longer matters

options.zh: 因为 scope 只适用于 management staff
options.en: Because scope applies only to managers

questions.explanationZh: 药理学不能脱离 scope of practice 来理解。
questions.explanationEn: Pharmacology cannot be separated from scope of practice.

questions.questionZh: 学完 Chapter 7 后最该形成的能力是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important ability to develop after Chapter 7?

options.zh: 从药理机制、给药途径、风险和适应场景去理解药物
options.en: To understand medications through mechanisms, routes, risks, and use contexts

options.zh: 只会背药名
options.en: To memorize drug names only

options.zh: 完全靠经验猜药效
options.en: To guess drug effects based purely on intuition

options.zh: 把所有药当成差不多
options.en: To treat all medications as essentially the same

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 7 的目标是让药理真正进入 clinical reasoning。
questions.explanationEn: The goal of Chapter 7 is to bring pharmacology into clinical reasoning.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 8 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 8?

options.zh: Vascular access and medication administration
options.en: Vascular access and medication administration

options.zh: Only vehicle operations
options.en: Only vehicle operations

options.zh: Only pediatric communication
options.en: Only pediatric communication

options.zh: Only obstetrics
options.en: Only obstetrics

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 把药理从理论拉到实际执行。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 brings pharmacology from theory into practical execution.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Chapter 8 在药理之后学习最合理？
questions.questionEn: Why is it logical to study Chapter 8 after pharmacology?

options.zh: 因为先懂药理，再懂如何把药和液体安全送进病人体内
options.en: Because you first learn pharmacology, then how to safely deliver medications and fluids into the patient

options.zh: 因为顺序没有意义
options.en: Because the sequence has no meaning

options.zh: 因为 Chapter 8 只讲数学
options.en: Because Chapter 8 is only about math

options.zh: 因为 Chapter 7 与 Chapter 8 无关
options.en: Because Chapter 7 is unrelated to Chapter 8

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 是 Chapter 7 的实操延伸。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 is the practical extension of Chapter 7.

questions.questionZh: Medical asepsis 在 Chapter 8 中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is medical asepsis important in Chapter 8?

options.zh: 因为 vascular access 和 medication administration 涉及感染风险与 patient safety
options.en: Because vascular access and medication administration involve infection risk and patient safety

options.zh: 因为无菌只在手术室重要
options.en: Because asepsis matters only in the operating room

options.zh: 因为 asepsis 与 IV 无关
options.en: Because asepsis is unrelated to IV care

options.zh: 因为只要快就好
options.en: Because speed is all that matters

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 把 asepsis 视为执行技术中的核心安全原则。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 treats asepsis as a core safety principle in execution skills.

questions.questionZh: Routine precautions and contaminated equipment disposal 最主要的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main significance of routine precautions and contaminated equipment disposal?

options.zh: 保护病人、提供者与系统环境
options.en: To protect the patient, provider, and care environment

options.zh: 只是行政流程
options.en: Only an administrative process

options.zh: 只在医院才要做
options.en: Only needed in hospitals

options.zh: 与 patient safety 无关
options.en: Unrelated to patient safety

questions.explanationZh: 这一部分是 Chapter 8 中感染控制与职业安全的延伸。
questions.explanationEn: This section extends infection control and occupational safety within Chapter 8.

questions.questionZh: Fluids and electrolytes 为什么在本章中关键？
questions.questionEn: Why are fluids and electrolytes important in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为液体治疗和灌注、平衡、给药都与之相关
options.en: Because fluid therapy, perfusion, balance, and medication administration all relate to them

options.zh: 因为它们只用于实验室
options.en: Because they only matter in labs

options.zh: 因为 EMS 不处理液体问题
options.en: Because EMS does not handle fluid issues

options.zh: 因为它们不会影响病人状态
options.en: Because they do not affect patient condition

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 把流体管理和临床状态连接起来。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 links fluid management with clinical state.

questions.questionZh: IV fluid composition 为什么需要理解？
questions.questionEn: Why is understanding IV fluid composition important?

options.zh: 因为不同液体有不同临床用途和风险
options.en: Because different fluids have different clinical uses and risks

options.zh: 因为所有液体都一样
options.en: Because all fluids are the same

options.zh: 因为只要能滴进去就行
options.en: Because any fluid is fine as long as it infuses

options.zh: 因为液体类型不影响 decision-making
options.en: Because fluid type does not affect decisions

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 强调液体不是“随便挂一瓶”那么简单。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 emphasizes that IV fluids are not interchangeable by default.

questions.questionZh: IV techniques and administration 这部分最核心在训练什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central training focus of IV techniques and administration?

options.zh: 安全、规范、有效地建立和管理血管通路
options.en: To safely, properly, and effectively establish and manage vascular access

options.zh: 只求速度，不管流程
options.en: Speed only, regardless of process

options.zh: 只背器械名称
options.en: Memorizing equipment names only

options.zh: 只在 hospital 才需要懂
options.en: Only useful in hospitals

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 的价值在于把通路建立变成安全专业动作。
questions.explanationEn: The value of Chapter 8 is turning vascular access into a safe professional skill.

questions.questionZh: Potential complications of IV therapy 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are the potential complications of IV therapy important?

options.zh: 因为 IV 不是零风险技术，识别并发症是安全的一部分
options.en: Because IV therapy is not risk-free, and recognizing complications is part of safe care

options.zh: 因为 complication 很少，不用学
options.en: Because complications are so rare they need not be studied

options.zh: 因为只和护士有关
options.en: Because they only matter to nurses

options.zh: 因为只要扎进去就结束了
options.en: Because once the line is placed, the job is done

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 要求你不仅会做，还要会发现做坏了什么。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 requires not only doing the procedure but recognizing when something has gone wrong.

questions.questionZh: Intraosseous infusion 在本章中的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the significance of intraosseous infusion in this chapter?

options.zh: 提供在某些情况下替代 vascular access 的重要路径
options.en: It provides an important alternative vascular access route in certain situations

options.zh: 它只是理论概念
options.en: It is only a theoretical concept

options.zh: 它永远优于 IV
options.en: It is always superior to IV

options.zh: 它只适用于长期门诊
options.en: It is only for long-term outpatient use

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 把 IO 当成特定场景下的重要替代方案。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 presents IO as an important alternative in specific scenarios.

questions.questionZh: Calculating fluid infusion rates 为什么不能忽视？
questions.questionEn: Why should calculating fluid infusion rates not be ignored?

options.zh: 因为给液速度会直接影响治疗效果与安全性
options.en: Because infusion rate directly affects treatment effectiveness and safety

options.zh: 因为大概估计就够了
options.en: Because a rough guess is enough

options.zh: 因为只在 pharmacy 才用得到
options.en: Because it is only relevant in pharmacies

options.zh: 因为病人不会受滴速影响
options.en: Because patients are unaffected by infusion rate

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 强调 calculation 是 patient safety 能力。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 emphasizes calculation as a patient safety skill.

questions.questionZh: Pediatric drug doses 为什么要被单独强调？
questions.questionEn: Why are pediatric drug doses emphasized separately?

options.zh: 因为儿童不是缩小版成人，剂量需要更精确考量
options.en: Because children are not simply small adults and dosing requires greater precision

options.zh: 因为儿童都用同一剂量
options.en: Because all children receive the same dose

options.zh: 因为 pediatrics 不用药
options.en: Because pediatrics does not involve medication

options.zh: 因为只在 ICU 需要儿童剂量
options.en: Because pediatric dosing is only relevant in ICUs

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 提醒你儿童给药风险更高，精度更重要。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 reminds you that pediatric dosing carries higher risk and requires greater precision.

questions.questionZh: Enteral / parenteral / inhalation routes 为什么要一起学？
questions.questionEn: Why are enteral, parenteral, and inhalation routes studied together?

options.zh: 因为 route 选择本身就是 medication decision 的一部分
options.en: Because route selection is itself part of medication decision-making

options.zh: 因为它们没有差别
options.en: Because they have no meaningful differences

options.zh: 因为 route 只和偏好有关
options.en: Because routes are only about preference

options.zh: 因为只要能给进去就行
options.en: Because any route is fine as long as the drug gets in

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 把不同给药途径和临床场景配对理解。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 8 teaches route selection in relation to clinical context.

questions.questionZh: Medication administration 在本章中的真正重点是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the true focus of medication administration in this chapter?

options.zh: 把正确药物以正确方式、正确剂量、正确时机、安全地给到病人
options.en: Delivering the right medication by the right route, dose, timing, and safety process

options.zh: 只把药推进去
options.en: Simply pushing the medication in

options.zh: 只要有医嘱就不用思考
options.en: If there is an order, no thinking is needed

options.zh: 药物 administration 不需要 reassessment
options.en: Medication administration does not require reassessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 的 medication administration 强调完整性，不是单一步骤。
questions.explanationEn: Medication administration in Chapter 8 emphasizes the whole safe process, not a single isolated step.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 8 学完后最该建立什么能力？
questions.questionEn: What is the key ability to build after Chapter 8?

options.zh: 把药理知识安全地转化成血管通路、给液和给药执行能力
options.en: To safely convert pharmacology knowledge into execution skills involving access, fluids, and medication administration

options.zh: 只会扎针
options.en: To only place IVs

options.zh: 完全靠记忆，不做计算
options.en: To rely on memory alone and avoid calculations

options.zh: 把执行和 assessment 完全分开
options.en: To separate execution completely from assessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 8 的真正价值是把“知道”变成“安全做到”。
questions.explanationEn: The true value of Chapter 8 is turning knowing into safe execution.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 9 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 9?

options.zh: Human development
options.en: Human development

options.zh: Cardiac arrest only
options.en: Cardiac arrest only

options.zh: Only trauma splinting
options.en: Only trauma splinting

options.zh: Only hazardous materials
options.en: Only hazardous materials

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 讲的是不同年龄阶段的人类发展。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 9 focuses on human development across age groups.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Human Development 会放进这本书前半段？
questions.questionEn: Why is Human Development placed in the earlier part of the book?

options.zh: 因为不同年龄层会影响 assessment、沟通、风险和临床表现
options.en: Because age stage affects assessment, communication, risk, and clinical presentation

options.zh: 因为它只和儿科有关
options.en: Because it only concerns pediatrics

options.zh: 因为它和 clinical work 没关系
options.en: Because it is unrelated to clinical work

options.zh: 因为只是背景知识
options.en: Because it is only background knowledge

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 是后面 pediatrics、geriatrics 以及 general assessment 的基础。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 9 is foundational for later pediatrics, geriatrics, and general assessment.

questions.questionZh: Infants 这一部分为什么需要单独理解？
questions.questionEn: Why must infants be understood as a distinct developmental group?

options.zh: 因为婴儿在生理、表达、需求和风险上都与成人不同
options.en: Because infants differ from adults in physiology, communication, needs, and risk

options.zh: 因为婴儿就是小一点的成人
options.en: Because infants are simply smaller adults

options.zh: 因为婴儿不需要 assessment
options.en: Because infants do not need assessment

options.zh: 因为 EMS 不会遇到婴儿病人
options.en: Because EMS does not encounter infant patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 强调 developmental stage 会改变你的 clinical approach。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 9 emphasizes that developmental stage changes your clinical approach.

questions.questionZh: Toddlers and preschoolers 在临床上为什么特别？
questions.questionEn: Why are toddlers and preschoolers clinically distinctive?

options.zh: 因为发展阶段会影响合作、表达和 assessment 策略
options.en: Because developmental stage influences cooperation, communication, and assessment strategy

options.zh: 因为他们和 school-age children 完全一样
options.en: Because they are identical to school-age children

options.zh: 因为只要家长在就不用评估他们
options.en: Because parental presence eliminates the need for assessment

options.zh: 因为他们不会出现急症
options.en: Because they do not experience emergencies

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 提醒你不同儿童阶段不是一个模板。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 9 reminds you that different child stages are not interchangeable.

questions.questionZh: School-age children 为什么不能和 adolescents 混为一谈？
questions.questionEn: Why should school-age children not be grouped together with adolescents?

options.zh: 因为发展水平、沟通模式和临床互动方式不同
options.en: Because developmental level, communication, and clinical interaction differ

options.zh: 因为他们都只是儿童，所以没有差别
options.en: Because they are both children, so there is no difference

options.zh: 因为 school-age children 不需要解释病情
options.en: Because school-age children need no explanation of illness

options.zh: 因为 adolescents 不属于 Chapter 9
options.en: Because adolescents are not part of Chapter 9

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 的价值之一，就是把不同年龄层拆开看。
questions.explanationEn: One value of Chapter 9 is that it separates developmental stages rather than collapsing them together.

questions.questionZh: Adolescents 在临床沟通上为什么常需要单独考虑？
questions.questionEn: Why do adolescents often require special consideration in clinical communication?

options.zh: 因为他们处于独特的发展阶段，沟通与自主性常更复杂
options.en: Because they are in a distinct developmental stage where communication and autonomy may be more complex

options.zh: 因为 adolescents 不需要被当作独立个体
options.en: Because adolescents need not be treated as distinct individuals

options.zh: 因为 adolescent patient 永远比成人更简单
options.en: Because adolescent patients are always simpler than adults

options.zh: 因为青春期和 clinical care 无关
options.en: Because adolescence is unrelated to clinical care

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 为后续 patient communication 和 sensitive topics 做铺垫。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 9 lays groundwork for later patient communication and sensitive-topic assessment.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Early / Middle / Late adults 也要在本章中区分？
questions.questionEn: Why are early, middle, and late adults also distinguished in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为成人群体也会随着年龄变化出现不同的生理与风险特点
options.en: Because adults also differ physiologically and in risk as they age

options.zh: 因为成人都一样，不需要区分
options.en: Because all adults are the same

options.zh: 因为只有儿童发展才重要
options.en: Because only child development matters

options.zh: 因为 late adults 只属于 geriatrics 章节
options.en: Because late adults belong only in the geriatrics chapter

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 9 的 development 不是只讲小孩，而是整个人生阶段。
questions.explanationEn: Development in Chapter 9 is not just about children but the life span as a whole.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 9 为什么会影响后面的 patient assessment？
questions.questionEn: Why does Chapter 9 affect later patient assessment?

options.zh: 因为不同年龄层的正常表现、沟通方式和风险图像不同
options.en: Because normal presentation, communication style, and risk profile differ by age stage

options.zh: 因为 assessment 与年龄无关
options.en: Because assessment is unrelated to age

options.zh: 因为只要 vitals 正常就不用管年龄
options.en: Because age does not matter if vital signs are normal

options.zh: 因为所有患者都能按同一方式评估
options.en: Because all patients can be assessed the same way

questions.explanationZh: 这章让你理解“同样的症状，在不同年龄层可能代表不同东西”。
questions.explanationEn: This chapter teaches that the same symptom may mean different things at different developmental stages.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 9 最不想让你留下的误解是什么？
questions.questionEn: What misunderstanding does Chapter 9 most want to prevent?

options.zh: 把所有病人都当成“成人模板”的变体
options.en: Treating all patients as variants of the adult template

options.zh: 认为发展差异很重要
options.en: Thinking developmental differences matter

options.zh: 年龄会影响评估和沟通
options.en: Age influences assessment and communication

options.zh: 人类发展是临床的一部分
options.en: Human development is part of clinical care

questions.explanationZh: 本章要打破的，就是“所有病人都按同一模型处理”的想法。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter aims to break the idea that every patient can be managed through the same model.

questions.questionZh: 学完 Chapter 9 最该建立什么底层观念？
questions.questionEn: What foundational idea should be built after Chapter 9?

options.zh: 不同年龄阶段的人，在临床上不是同一个模板的缩放版
options.en: Different age groups are not simply scaled versions of one template in clinical care

options.zh: 年龄只是 demographic 信息，不影响 care
options.en: Age is just demographic information and does not affect care

options.zh: 只有儿童才需要特殊思维
options.en: Only children require special thinking

options.zh: 发展阶段与 EMS 无关
options.en: Developmental stage is unrelated to EMS

questions.explanationZh: 这是 Chapter 9 最核心的 take-away。
questions.explanationEn: This is the core take-away of Chapter 9.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 10 的核心主题是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core theme of Chapter 10?

options.zh: Patient communication
options.en: Patient communication

options.zh: Only trauma triage
options.en: Only trauma triage

options.zh: Only ECG interpretation
options.en: Only ECG interpretation

options.zh: Only obstetric delivery
options.en: Only obstetric delivery

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 专门讲病人沟通与访谈。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 focuses specifically on patient communication and interviewing.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 Patient Communication 在这本书里占一整章？
questions.questionEn: Why does Patient Communication deserve a full chapter in this book?

options.zh: 因为很多 assessment 的质量取决于你能否有效沟通
options.en: Because the quality of assessment often depends on whether you can communicate effectively

options.zh: 因为沟通只影响礼貌，不影响 clinical care
options.en: Because communication only affects politeness, not clinical care

options.zh: 因为沟通只在 hospital 才重要
options.en: Because communication only matters in hospitals

options.zh: 因为 paramedic 不需要建立 rapport
options.en: Because paramedics do not need to build rapport

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 的重点是：你拿到的信息质量，很大程度取决于沟通。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 emphasizes that the quality of information you obtain depends heavily on communication.

questions.questionZh: Internal factors for effective communication 最重要在提醒什么？
questions.questionEn: What do internal factors for effective communication mainly remind us of?

options.zh: 沟通不仅取决于对方，也取决于提供者自身状态与方式
options.en: Communication depends not only on the other person, but also on the provider’s own state and manner

options.zh: 只要病人会说话就等于沟通成功
options.en: If the patient can talk, communication is automatically successful

options.zh: 沟通完全不受自身影响
options.en: Communication is completely unaffected by the provider

options.zh: 只靠话术模板就够了
options.en: Templates alone are sufficient

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 不是把沟通看成单向动作，而是双向互动。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 treats communication as a two-way interaction, not a one-way act.

questions.questionZh: External factors for effective communication 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are external factors for effective communication important?

options.zh: 因为环境、噪音、家属、现场氛围等会影响信息获取与合作
options.en: Because environment, noise, family presence, and scene atmosphere affect information gathering and cooperation

options.zh: 因为外部环境和沟通无关
options.en: Because the external environment does not affect communication

options.zh: 因为只要有经验就能忽略环境
options.en: Because experience makes the environment irrelevant

options.zh: 因为沟通只发生在安静房间里
options.en: Because communication only occurs in quiet rooms

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 把现场环境也纳入 communication thinking。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 includes the scene environment in communication thinking.

questions.questionZh: Developing rapport 的真正作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the true role of developing rapport?

options.zh: 帮助建立信任、合作与更高质量的信息交换
options.en: To build trust, cooperation, and higher-quality information exchange

options.zh: 只是让病人喜欢你
options.en: Only to make the patient like you

options.zh: 和 assessment 无关
options.en: It is unrelated to assessment

options.zh: 只适用于低 acuity case
options.en: It only applies to low-acuity cases

questions.explanationZh: rapport 不是客套，而是 clinical function。
questions.explanationEn: Rapport is not just courtesy; it has a clinical function.

questions.questionZh: Conducting the interview 这一部分最重要在训练什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important skill trained in the conducting the interview section?

options.zh: 如何有结构地获取真正有用的病史信息
options.en: How to obtain truly useful history in a structured way

options.zh: 如何问得越多越好
options.en: How to ask as many questions as possible

options.zh: 如何完全照表念
options.en: How to rigidly read a script

options.zh: 如何只问主诉不问背景
options.en: How to ask only the chief complaint and ignore context

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 不追求问题数量，而追求有效信息。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 does not prioritize the number of questions, but the value of information gathered.

questions.questionZh: Common interviewing errors 为什么要单独讲？
questions.questionEn: Why are common interviewing errors taught as a separate topic?

options.zh: 因为错误沟通会直接损害 assessment 与 decision-making
options.en: Because poor interviewing can directly damage assessment and decision-making

options.zh: 因为错误只影响态度，不影响信息质量
options.en: Because mistakes affect attitude only, not information quality

options.zh: 因为 interviewing error 很少见
options.en: Because interviewing errors are rare

options.zh: 因为只要病人愿意说就不会出错
options.en: Because no errors occur if the patient is willing to talk

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 认为 interviewing error 是 clinical error 的来源之一。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 treats interviewing errors as one source of clinical error.

questions.questionZh: Nonverbal skills 在本章中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are nonverbal skills important in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为沟通不只靠语言，姿态、表情、距离和语气也会影响互动
options.en: Because communication is not only verbal; posture, expression, distance, and tone also shape the interaction

options.zh: 因为 nonverbal 完全不重要
options.en: Because nonverbal communication does not matter

options.zh: 因为只要问对问题就够了
options.en: Because correct questions alone are enough

options.zh: 因为 nonverbal 只适用于心理病人
options.en: Because nonverbal communication matters only in psychiatric cases

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 把 nonverbal communication 当成真正的评估工具之一。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 treats nonverbal communication as one of the real tools of assessment.

questions.questionZh: Assessing mental status 为什么会放进 Patient Communication？
questions.questionEn: Why is assessing mental status included in Patient Communication?

options.zh: 因为病人的认知与精神状态会直接影响沟通质量和信息可靠性
options.en: Because cognition and mental status directly affect communication quality and information reliability

options.zh: 因为 mental status 只属于 neurologic chapter
options.en: Because mental status belongs only in the neurologic chapter

options.zh: 因为沟通和 mental status 无关
options.en: Because communication is unrelated to mental status

options.zh: 因为只要病人清醒就不需要观察 mental status
options.en: Because if the patient is awake, mental status does not need assessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 把 mental status 放进沟通链里理解，而不是完全分开。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 10 integrates mental status into the communication framework rather than isolating it completely.

questions.questionZh: Special interview situations 这部分最重要在提醒什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main reminder in the special interview situations section?

options.zh: 不是所有病人都能用同一套访谈方式应对
options.en: Not every patient can be approached with the same interviewing style

options.zh: 特殊场景只要问得更快就行
options.en: In special situations, the answer is simply to ask faster

options.zh: 所有场景都可以用同一个模板
options.en: One template works for all situations

options.zh: 只要 family 在就不需要适应访谈方式
options.en: If family is present, no adaptation is needed

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 的核心之一就是 flexible communication。
questions.explanationEn: One of the core ideas in Chapter 10 is flexible communication.

questions.questionZh: 学完 Chapter 10 最该建立什么能力？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important ability to build after Chapter 10?

options.zh: 把沟通当成 assessment 的一部分，而不是附属礼貌动作
options.en: To treat communication as part of assessment rather than a separate courtesy skill

options.zh: 把沟通和 clinical work 完全分开
options.en: To separate communication completely from clinical work

options.zh: 认为只靠技术就能拿到全部信息
options.en: To assume technical skill alone gives all the needed information

options.zh: 尽量减少和病人的对话
options.en: To minimize conversation with patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 10 最重要的收获之一，就是把 communication 看成 clinical function。
questions.explanationEn: One of the most important takeaways from Chapter 10 is seeing communication as a clinical function.

questions.questionZh: 药理学里，pharmacokinetics 主要研究什么？
questions.questionEn: In pharmacology, what does pharmacokinetics primarily study?

options.zh: 药物对机体产生什么作用
options.en: What the drug does to the body

options.zh: 机体如何吸收、分布、代谢和排泄药物
options.en: How the body absorbs, distributes, metabolizes, and eliminates a drug

options.zh: 药物包装颜色
options.en: The color of the drug packaging

options.zh: 医院收费标准
options.en: Hospital billing standards

questions.explanationZh: pharmacokinetics 关注 ADME：吸收、分布、代谢、排泄。
questions.explanationEn: Pharmacokinetics focuses on ADME: absorption, distribution, metabolism, and elimination.

questions.questionZh: pharmacodynamics 最贴近哪种描述？
questions.questionEn: Which description best matches pharmacodynamics?

options.zh: 药物如何被身体处理
options.en: How the body handles the drug

options.zh: 药物对受体和机体产生的作用
options.en: How the drug acts on receptors and the body

options.zh: 药品库存管理
options.en: Medication inventory management

options.zh: 给药器械清单
options.en: A list of medication devices

questions.explanationZh: pharmacodynamics 研究药物对身体“做了什么”。
questions.explanationEn: Pharmacodynamics studies what the drug does to the body.

questions.questionZh: 哪一项最能说明给药前核对的重要性？
questions.questionEn: What best explains the importance of checking a medication before administration?

options.zh: 可以减少错误药物、错误剂量和错误病人的风险
options.en: It reduces the risk of wrong medication, wrong dose, and wrong patient errors

options.zh: 只是为了让流程看起来正式
options.en: It only makes the process look formal

options.zh: 主要为了拖延时间
options.en: It mainly helps delay treatment

options.zh: 只适用于口服药
options.en: It only applies to oral drugs

questions.explanationZh: 药理和给药安全的核心就是在给药前核对病人、药物、剂量、途径和时机。
questions.explanationEn: A core principle of medication safety is checking the patient, medication, dose, route, and timing before administration.

questions.questionZh: 如果一种药物会和另一种药互相影响，最相关的概念是？
questions.questionEn: If one medication affects another medication, which concept is most relevant?

options.zh: drug interaction
options.en: Drug interaction

options.zh: vital sign trend
options.en: Vital sign trend

options.zh: scene size-up
options.en: Scene size-up

options.zh: body mechanics
options.en: Body mechanics

questions.explanationZh: 药物之间彼此影响疗效或副作用时，就是 drug interaction。
questions.explanationEn: When medications influence each other’s effects or side effects, that is a drug interaction.

questions.questionZh: 建立 vascular access 的主要目的之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one major purpose of establishing vascular access?

options.zh: 便于给予液体和药物
options.en: To provide fluids and medications

options.zh: 让病人更紧张
options.en: To make the patient more anxious

options.zh: 只是为了练习技术
options.en: Only to practice a skill

options.zh: 替代所有病史采集
options.en: To replace taking a history

questions.explanationZh: vascular access 的核心用途之一就是液体和药物给入。
questions.explanationEn: A core purpose of vascular access is the administration of fluids and medications.

questions.questionZh: 关于 medication administration，哪项最正确？
questions.questionEn: Which statement about medication administration is most correct?

options.zh: 给药途径会影响起效速度和效果
options.en: The route of administration affects onset and effect

options.zh: 所有药物无论途径起效都一样
options.en: All medications act the same regardless of route

options.zh: 吸入给药永远最慢
options.en: Inhaled medications are always the slowest

options.zh: IV 给药不需要任何监测
options.en: IV medication requires no monitoring

questions.explanationZh: 不同给药途径会影响起效、吸收和临床效果。
questions.explanationEn: Different routes of administration affect onset, absorption, and clinical effect.

questions.questionZh: 哪一项属于 parenteral route？
questions.questionEn: Which of the following is a parenteral route?

options.zh: IV
options.en: IV

options.zh: PO
options.en: PO

options.zh: SL only
options.en: SL only

options.zh: Topical patch only
options.en: Topical patch only

questions.explanationZh: parenteral 一般指绕过消化道，如 IV、IM、SC、IO 等。
questions.explanationEn: Parenteral routes bypass the digestive tract, such as IV, IM, SC, and IO.

questions.questionZh: 在药物计算里，最危险的错误之一通常是什么？
questions.questionEn: In medication calculation, what is often one of the most dangerous errors?

options.zh: 剂量换算错误
options.en: A dose conversion error

options.zh: 字写得太慢
options.en: Writing too slowly

options.zh: 问病人问题太多
options.en: Asking too many questions

options.zh: 担架太重
options.en: A heavy stretcher

questions.explanationZh: 药量、浓度和体重换算错误会直接导致过量或不足量给药。
questions.explanationEn: Errors in dose, concentration, or weight-based calculations can directly cause over- or under-dosing.

questions.questionZh: Human Development 这一章的核心意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core importance of the Human Development chapter?

options.zh: 提醒你不同年龄层不是同一个病人的缩小或放大版
options.en: It teaches that different age groups are not simply smaller or larger versions of the same patient

options.zh: 只讨论药品库存
options.en: It only discusses medication stock

options.zh: 只讨论创伤中心分级
options.en: It only discusses trauma center levels

options.zh: 只讨论 documentation
options.en: It only discusses documentation

questions.explanationZh: 不同年龄层在解剖、生理、心理和沟通上都有显著差异。
questions.explanationEn: Different age groups have important differences in anatomy, physiology, psychology, and communication.

questions.questionZh: 儿科病人 assessment 中，一个常见原则是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is a common principle in pediatric patient assessment?

options.zh: 尽量减少威胁感并观察家属互动
options.en: Minimize the child’s sense of threat and observe caregiver interaction

options.zh: 一开始就尽量制造压力测试反应
options.en: Create stress immediately to test the child’s response

options.zh: 完全忽略发育阶段
options.en: Ignore developmental stage completely

options.zh: 默认儿童沟通方式和成人完全相同
options.en: Assume children communicate exactly like adults

questions.explanationZh: 儿科 assessment 常强调环境、照护者互动和发育阶段。
questions.explanationEn: Pediatric assessment often emphasizes environment, caregiver interaction, and developmental stage.

questions.questionZh: 老年病人的一个常见 clinical consideration 是？
questions.questionEn: What is a common clinical consideration in geriatric patients?

options.zh: 可能有多病共存和非典型表现
options.en: They may have multiple comorbidities and atypical presentations

options.zh: 通常完全没有基础病
options.en: They usually have no baseline illness

options.zh: 一定能清楚表达全部症状
options.en: They will always clearly express all symptoms

options.zh: 对药物反应完全一致
options.en: Their drug responses are always identical

questions.explanationZh: 老年患者常有多病共存、用药复杂和症状不典型。
questions.explanationEn: Older adults often have multiple comorbidities, complex medication profiles, and atypical presentations.

questions.questionZh: 青春期病人的沟通通常需要特别注意什么？
questions.questionEn: Communication with adolescent patients often requires special attention to what?

options.zh: 隐私、尊重和自主感
options.en: Privacy, respect, and a sense of autonomy

options.zh: 只和家属说话
options.en: Speaking only with the family

options.zh: 完全跳过病史
options.en: Skipping the history entirely

options.zh: 先讨论费用
options.en: Discussing fees first

questions.explanationZh: 青少年常特别在意隐私、尊重和被当成独立个体对待。
questions.explanationEn: Adolescents often care strongly about privacy, respect, and being treated as individuals.

questions.questionZh: Patient Communication 的核心目标之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one core goal of patient communication?

options.zh: 建立 rapport 并获得可靠信息
options.en: To build rapport and obtain reliable information

options.zh: 尽量减少与病人说话
options.en: To minimize speaking with the patient

options.zh: 只说专业术语以显得专业
options.en: To use only jargon so you sound professional

options.zh: 避免任何非语言沟通
options.en: To avoid all nonverbal communication

questions.explanationZh: 良好沟通不仅是礼貌，更直接影响 assessment 质量。
questions.explanationEn: Good communication is not just polite; it directly affects assessment quality.

questions.questionZh: 哪项最能帮助改善 patient interview？
questions.questionEn: What most helps improve a patient interview?

options.zh: 开放式问题与积极倾听
options.en: Open-ended questions and active listening

options.zh: 频繁打断病人
options.en: Frequently interrupting the patient

options.zh: 只问是非题
options.en: Asking only yes/no questions

options.zh: 忽视病人的情绪
options.en: Ignoring the patient’s emotions

questions.explanationZh: 开放式提问配合积极倾听，通常更容易获得完整病史。
questions.explanationEn: Open-ended questions combined with active listening usually lead to a more complete history.

questions.questionZh: 非语言讯号在沟通中的价值是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the value of nonverbal cues in communication?

options.zh: 它们可以补充或修正语言内容
options.en: They can reinforce or modify the meaning of spoken words

options.zh: 完全没有价值
options.en: They have no value

options.zh: 只在法庭上有用
options.en: They are only useful in court

options.zh: 只适用于儿童
options.en: They apply only to children

questions.explanationZh: 表情、姿势、语气等常提供重要临床和情绪线索。
questions.explanationEn: Facial expression, posture, and tone often provide important clinical and emotional clues.

questions.questionZh: 病人情绪激动时，哪种做法更合适？
questions.questionEn: When a patient is emotionally upset, which approach is more appropriate?

options.zh: 保持冷静、明确、尊重并提供 reassurance
options.en: Remain calm, clear, respectful, and provide reassurance

options.zh: 提高音量压过对方
options.en: Raise your voice to overpower the patient

options.zh: 立刻结束沟通
options.en: End the conversation immediately

options.zh: 假装没看到情绪变化
options.en: Pretend you did not notice the emotional change

questions.explanationZh: 在高压力场景中，稳定情绪和建立信任往往先于更深入的问诊。
questions.explanationEn: In high-stress situations, emotional stabilization and trust-building often come before deeper questioning.

questions.questionZh: Research and Quality 这一章最强调哪种思维？
questions.questionEn: What type of thinking is emphasized in Research and Quality?

options.zh: evidence-based thinking
options.en: Evidence-based thinking

options.zh: 完全凭直觉
options.en: Pure intuition with no evidence

options.zh: 只照旧习惯做
options.en: Doing things only because they were done that way before

options.zh: 避免任何质量评估
options.en: Avoiding all quality review

questions.explanationZh: 研究与质量管理的核心是用证据改进 patient care 与系统表现。
questions.explanationEn: The core of research and quality is using evidence to improve patient care and system performance.

questions.questionZh: Quality improvement 的主要目的是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main purpose of quality improvement?

options.zh: 持续改进病人安全和系统表现
options.en: To continuously improve patient safety and system performance

options.zh: 单纯惩罚个人
options.en: To punish individuals

options.zh: 减少所有 documentation
options.en: To reduce all documentation

options.zh: 让流程更复杂
options.en: To make processes more complicated

questions.explanationZh: QI 的目标是发现问题、分析原因并改进流程和结果。
questions.explanationEn: The goal of quality improvement is to identify problems, analyze causes, and improve processes and outcomes.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 paramedic 需要理解基础 research design？
questions.questionEn: Why do paramedics need to understand basic research design?

options.zh: 这样才能判断证据质量并更好应用到实践
options.en: So they can judge the quality of evidence and apply it more effectively in practice

options.zh: 只是为了考试记术语
options.en: Only to memorize terminology for exams

options.zh: 因为这样就不用问医嘱
options.en: Because then they never need medical direction

options.zh: 与临床完全无关
options.en: Because it is unrelated to clinical work

questions.explanationZh: 理解研究设计有助于分辨高质量证据和低质量结论。
questions.explanationEn: Understanding research design helps clinicians distinguish stronger evidence from weaker conclusions.

questions.questionZh: 病人安全文化最贴近哪种描述？
questions.questionEn: Which description best fits a culture of patient safety?

options.zh: 鼓励识别风险、报告问题并从错误中学习
options.en: Encouraging risk recognition, problem reporting, and learning from errors

options.zh: 隐藏问题避免被发现
options.en: Hiding problems so they are not discovered

options.zh: 只看速度不看结果
options.en: Focusing only on speed, never outcomes

options.zh: 把所有差错都归咎于运气
options.en: Blaming every error on bad luck

questions.explanationZh: patient safety culture 强调透明、学习和系统改进。
questions.explanationEn: A patient-safety culture emphasizes transparency, learning, and system improvement.

questions.questionZh: Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada 这一章的重点是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the focus of Expanded EMS Systems and Community Paramedicine in Canada?

options.zh: EMS 角色扩展到社区与长期健康支持
options.en: The expansion of EMS roles into community and longer-term health support

options.zh: 只讨论航空医学
options.en: It focuses only on aerospace medicine

options.zh: 只讨论外科器械清点
options.en: It focuses only on surgical instrument counts

options.zh: 只讨论火场救援
options.en: It focuses only on fire-ground rescue

questions.explanationZh: community paramedicine 关注 EMS 如何超越单次转运，进入社区支持与系统整合。
questions.explanationEn: Community paramedicine examines how EMS can extend beyond one-time transport into community support and system integration.

questions.questionZh: community paramedicine 的一个潜在价值是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one potential value of community paramedicine?

options.zh: 帮助减少不必要转运并改善持续照护连接
options.en: Helping reduce unnecessary transports and improving continuity of care

options.zh: 完全取代所有医院
options.en: Replacing all hospitals entirely

options.zh: 不再需要 assessment
options.en: Eliminating the need for assessment

options.zh: 只增加 paperwork 而没有临床意义
options.en: Only adding paperwork without clinical value

questions.explanationZh: 这类模式常强调 prevention、follow-up 和资源连接。
questions.explanationEn: These models often emphasize prevention, follow-up, and connecting patients with appropriate resources.

questions.questionZh: nontransport 争议为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the issue of nontransport important?

options.zh: 因为它涉及病人安全、适应证判断和系统责任
options.en: Because it involves patient safety, deciding when it is appropriate, and system responsibility

options.zh: 因为它和 EMS 无关
options.en: Because it has nothing to do with EMS

options.zh: 因为所有病人都必须永远 nontransport
options.en: Because every patient should always be nontransported

options.zh: 因为它只和收费有关
options.en: Because it is only about billing

questions.explanationZh: nontransport 不是简单的“不送”，而是涉及风险、评估和系统规范。
questions.explanationEn: Nontransport is not simply “not transporting”; it involves risk, assessment quality, and system standards.

questions.questionZh: 这章帮助学习者看到 EMS 的哪种变化？
questions.questionEn: This chapter helps learners see what broader change in EMS?

options.zh: EMS 不只是“接了送医院”，而是系统中的更广泛健康角色
options.en: EMS is not only about picking up and transporting, but about a broader role in the health system

options.zh: EMS 将完全取消急救功能
options.en: EMS will completely abandon emergency care

options.zh: EMS 只剩 paperwork
options.en: EMS will be reduced to paperwork only

options.zh: EMS 以后只做院内工作
options.en: EMS will work only inside hospitals

questions.explanationZh: 这一章的意义在于拓宽 EMS 的系统视野。
questions.explanationEn: The chapter broadens the learner’s understanding of EMS as part of a wider health system role.

questions.questionZh: 在 airway management 中，最核心的优先问题之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: In airway management, what is one of the most fundamental priority questions?

options.zh: 病人能否维持通畅气道
options.en: Whether the patient can maintain a patent airway

options.zh: 病人最喜欢哪种面罩颜色
options.en: Which mask color the patient prefers

options.zh: 今天的天气如何
options.en: What the weather is like

options.zh: 担架品牌是什么
options.en: What brand the stretcher is

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 的核心是先判断病人能不能维持气道并有效通气氧合。
questions.explanationEn: A core task in Chapter 13 is determining whether the patient can maintain the airway and ventilate/oxygenate effectively.

questions.questionZh: 哪种情况最提示需要 airway adjunct 或进一步 airway support？
questions.questionEn: Which finding most strongly suggests the need for an airway adjunct or further airway support?

options.zh: 意识下降且无法良好保护气道
options.en: Decreased LOC with poor airway protection

options.zh: 轻度皮肤瘙痒
options.en: Mild itchy skin

options.zh: 既往扭伤史
options.en: A prior sprain history

options.zh: 今天没吃早餐
options.en: Not having eaten breakfast

questions.explanationZh: 当病人因意识下降无法保护气道时，就要考虑 airway adjunct 或更高级气道支持。
questions.explanationEn: When a patient cannot protect the airway because of decreased LOC, airway adjuncts or more advanced support may be needed.

questions.questionZh: oxygenation 与 ventilation 的关系，哪项最准确？
questions.questionEn: Which statement most accurately describes the relationship between oxygenation and ventilation?

options.zh: 两者完全一样
options.en: They are exactly the same

options.zh: 有氧气就一定通气良好
options.en: If oxygen is present, ventilation must be adequate

options.zh: 两者相关但并不相同
options.en: They are related but not identical

options.zh: 只能先考虑其中一个
options.en: Only one should ever be considered at a time

questions.explanationZh: 氧合与通气密切相关，但一个正常不代表另一个一定正常。
questions.explanationEn: Oxygenation and ventilation are closely related, but one can be impaired even when the other seems acceptable.

questions.questionZh: 吸引（suctioning）在什么场景最重要？
questions.questionEn: Suctioning is most important in what situation?

options.zh: 上气道有分泌物、血液或呕吐物可能造成阻塞时
options.en: When secretions, blood, or vomit may obstruct the upper airway

options.zh: 单纯手腕疼痛时
options.en: When the patient only has wrist pain

options.zh: 想让病人安静一点时
options.en: When you want the patient to be quieter

options.zh: 所有稳定病人都常规进行时
options.en: Routinely for all stable patients

questions.explanationZh: suctioning 的典型用途是清除可能阻塞上气道的液体或分泌物。
questions.explanationEn: The classic indication for suctioning is clearing fluids or secretions that threaten upper-airway patency.

questions.questionZh: Mechanical Ventilation 这一章的核心，是把什么提升到更高层级？
questions.questionEn: The core of Mechanical Ventilation is elevating what to a higher level?

options.zh: 基础通气支持
options.en: Basic ventilatory support

options.zh: 病历收费流程
options.en: Billing workflow

options.zh: 骨折固定技术
options.en: Fracture splinting techniques

options.zh: 创伤分流规则
options.en: Trauma triage rules

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 14 是从基础通气过渡到机械通气与更高级呼吸支持。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 14 builds on basic ventilation and moves into mechanical ventilation and higher-level respiratory support.

questions.questionZh: 使用机械通气时，为什么持续监测很重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is ongoing monitoring so important during mechanical ventilation?

options.zh: 因为病人的通气状态和对通气的反应可能变化
options.en: Because the patient’s ventilation status and response can change

options.zh: 只是为了让设备看起来更高级
options.en: Only to make the equipment look more advanced

options.zh: 因为通气一开始就永远不会出问题
options.en: Because ventilation never causes problems once started

options.zh: 只为了记录一个数字
options.en: Only to record a single number

questions.explanationZh: 机械通气不是设好就结束，必须持续看病人反应、氧合和通气效果。
questions.explanationEn: Mechanical ventilation is not “set and forget”; the patient’s response, oxygenation, and ventilation must be monitored continuously.

questions.questionZh: Mechanical ventilation 相关 assessment 最不应该忽略哪一项？
questions.questionEn: Which item should not be ignored in mechanical-ventilation assessment?

options.zh: 病人的临床表现与监测趋势
options.en: The patient’s clinical presentation and monitoring trends

options.zh: 设备颜色
options.en: The color of the machine

options.zh: 房间灯光
options.en: The room lighting

options.zh: 操作者心情
options.en: The operator’s mood

questions.explanationZh: 无论设备参数如何，病人的临床表现始终最重要。
questions.explanationEn: Regardless of the machine settings, the patient’s clinical presentation remains most important.

questions.questionZh: 在 Chapter 14 的学习逻辑里，呼吸机管理属于哪类能力？
questions.questionEn: In the learning logic of Chapter 14, ventilator management belongs to what kind of skill set?

options.zh: 高级呼吸支持与监测能力
options.en: Advanced respiratory-support and monitoring skills

options.zh: 单纯法律记录能力
options.en: Pure legal documentation skills

options.zh: 骨科专科操作
options.en: Orthopedic specialty procedures

options.zh: 现场火灾控制能力
options.en: Fire-scene control skills

questions.explanationZh: Mechanical ventilation 是更高层次的呼吸支持内容。
questions.explanationEn: Mechanical ventilation is part of higher-level respiratory support.

questions.questionZh: Patient History 这一章最核心的目标是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central goal of the Patient History chapter?

options.zh: 从病人、家属和现场获得关键线索
options.en: To gather key clues from the patient, family, and scene

options.zh: 减少与病人的交流
options.en: To reduce communication with the patient

options.zh: 只记录生命体征
options.en: To record vital signs only

options.zh: 直接跳过问诊
options.en: To skip questioning entirely

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 15 的重点是通过 history 获取对病情判断有价值的信息。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 15 focuses on obtaining information that meaningfully supports clinical judgment.

questions.questionZh: 哪种提问方式通常更有助于病人完整描述主诉？
questions.questionEn: Which questioning style usually helps patients describe their complaint more fully?

options.zh: 开放式问题
options.en: Open-ended questions

options.zh: 只问“是不是”
options.en: Only yes/no questions

options.zh: 完全不让病人自己说
options.en: Not letting the patient speak freely at all

options.zh: 用最快速度打断病人
options.en: Interrupting the patient as quickly as possible

questions.explanationZh: 开放式问题常帮助病人更完整地表达主诉与病程。
questions.explanationEn: Open-ended questions often help patients describe the complaint and illness course more completely.

questions.questionZh: 敏感病史问题的处理原则，哪项更合适？
questions.questionEn: Which approach is more appropriate for sensitive history questions?

options.zh: 尊重、清晰并尽量保护隐私
options.en: Be respectful, clear, and protect privacy as much as possible

options.zh: 在围观人群中大声询问
options.en: Ask loudly in front of bystanders

options.zh: 完全不问
options.en: Never ask at all

options.zh: 故意让病人难堪
options.en: Intentionally embarrass the patient

questions.explanationZh: 敏感问题仍然要问，但方式应尊重并注意隐私。
questions.explanationEn: Sensitive questions may still be necessary, but they should be asked respectfully and with attention to privacy.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 history 不能只依赖病人一句主诉？
questions.questionEn: Why can’t history taking rely on the chief complaint alone?

options.zh: 因为现病史、既往史、相关症状和背景常改变临床判断
options.en: Because history of present illness, prior history, associated symptoms, and context often change clinical judgment

options.zh: 因为主诉永远不重要
options.en: Because the chief complaint is never important

options.zh: 因为只需要 physical exam
options.en: Because only the physical exam matters

options.zh: 因为 documentation 不允许写主诉
options.en: Because documentation does not allow the chief complaint

questions.explanationZh: 主诉只是入口，真正判断还要靠病程、相关症状和背景。
questions.explanationEn: The chief complaint is only the starting point; decision-making depends on the illness course, associated symptoms, and context.

questions.questionZh: Physical Examination 这一章的核心是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core of the Physical Examination chapter?

options.zh: 系统地看、摸、听、测并组织 findings
options.en: Systematically inspect, palpate, auscultate, measure, and organize findings

options.zh: 只看 monitor 数字
options.en: Only look at monitor numbers

options.zh: 尽量不碰病人
options.en: Avoid touching the patient whenever possible

options.zh: 完全依赖家属描述
options.en: Rely entirely on family description

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 16 的重点是把查体做系统并转化成可用临床信息。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 16 focuses on performing a systematic physical exam and turning findings into usable clinical information.

questions.questionZh: general impression 在 physical exam 中为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is the general impression important in the physical exam?

options.zh: 它常在详细检查前就提供病人病重程度的早期线索
options.en: It often provides early clues to acuity before the detailed exam

options.zh: 它只是一种礼貌动作
options.en: It is only a polite gesture

options.zh: 它和病情完全无关
options.en: It has nothing to do with acuity

options.zh: 它只适用于书面考试
options.en: It applies only to written exams

questions.explanationZh: general impression 常常在第一眼就帮助判断 sick or not sick。
questions.explanationEn: A general impression often helps determine “sick or not sick” within the first moments of contact.

questions.questionZh: 生命体征在 physical examination 中的作用，哪项最准确？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best describes the role of vital signs in the physical examination?

options.zh: 它们为临床判断和趋势评估提供客观数据
options.en: They provide objective data for clinical judgment and trend assessment

options.zh: 只要第一次正常，以后就不用再测
options.en: If normal once, they never need reassessment

options.zh: 它们比病人表现更重要到可以完全替代观察
options.en: They are so important they can replace direct observation

options.zh: 只用于 documentation，不影响决策
options.en: They are only for documentation and do not affect decisions

questions.explanationZh: 生命体征不仅要看一次，还要结合趋势和整体表现理解。
questions.explanationEn: Vital signs are not just one-time numbers; they should be interpreted in context and as trends.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 documentation 与查体紧密相关？
questions.questionEn: Why is documentation closely tied to the physical exam?

options.zh: 因为 findings 只有被准确记录和传达，才真正能支持后续照护
options.en: Because findings only support care effectively when they are accurately recorded and communicated

options.zh: 因为 documentation 可以替代 exam
options.en: Because documentation can replace the exam

options.zh: 因为 exam 不需要任何记录
options.en: Because exams never need to be recorded

options.zh: 因为只有法律才关心查体
options.en: Because only legal staff care about the exam

questions.explanationZh: 查体结果要能被记录、传达和复盘，才算真正完成。
questions.explanationEn: Physical-exam findings must be documented and communicated to fully support ongoing care.

questions.questionZh: Patient Assessment 这一章最核心的作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central role of the Patient Assessment chapter?

options.zh: 把 history 和 physical exam 串成完整临床流程
options.en: To integrate history and physical exam into a complete clinical process

options.zh: 只教药物剂量
options.en: To teach drug doses only

options.zh: 只讨论法律争议
options.en: To discuss legal disputes only

options.zh: 只讨论社区项目
options.en: To discuss community programs only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 的意义就是把病史、查体、scene assessment 和 ongoing assessment 整合起来。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 17 integrates history, physical exam, scene assessment, and ongoing assessment into one coherent process.

questions.questionZh: 在 patient assessment 中，scene assessment 的价值是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the value of scene assessment in patient assessment?

options.zh: 它在接触病人前就提供环境、机制和安全线索
options.en: It provides environmental, mechanism, and safety clues before direct patient contact

options.zh: 它只适用于 written exam
options.en: It only matters for written exams

options.zh: 它可以取代全部病史和查体
options.en: It can replace all history and physical exam

options.zh: 它和病人病情无关
options.en: It has nothing to do with patient condition

questions.explanationZh: 现场评估帮助判断危险、机制、资源需求和病人严重程度。
questions.explanationEn: Scene assessment helps determine hazards, mechanism, resource needs, and likely acuity.

questions.questionZh: ongoing assessment 为什么重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is ongoing assessment important?

options.zh: 因为病人的状态会变化，计划也可能需要调整
options.en: Because patient status can change and the care plan may need to be adjusted

options.zh: 因为只要做过一次 assessment 就够了
options.en: Because one assessment is always enough

options.zh: 因为只是形式要求
options.en: Because it is only a formal requirement

options.zh: 因为它只对 documentation 有意义
options.en: Because it only matters for documentation

questions.explanationZh: 病人不是静止的，ongoing assessment 让你能及时发现恶化或改善。
questions.explanationEn: Patients are dynamic; ongoing assessment lets you identify deterioration or improvement in time.

questions.questionZh: focused exam 最适合哪种用途？
questions.questionEn: What is a focused exam most useful for?

options.zh: 针对主诉或高风险系统做更有方向性的检查
options.en: To perform a more directed exam based on the complaint or high-risk system

options.zh: 完全跳过初步评估
options.en: To skip the initial assessment completely

options.zh: 只用于法医目的
options.en: Only for forensic purposes

options.zh: 替代所有生命体征
options.en: To replace all vital signs

questions.explanationZh: focused exam 的重点是有方向地缩小问题范围。
questions.explanationEn: A focused exam narrows attention to the complaint or highest-yield system.

questions.questionZh: Clinical Decisions 这一章最想培养什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the main skill the Clinical Decisions chapter aims to build?

options.zh: critical thinking and judgment
options.en: Critical thinking and judgment

options.zh: 只会背步骤
options.en: Only step memorization

options.zh: 只会写病历
options.en: Only chart writing

options.zh: 只会设备操作
options.en: Only equipment operation

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 18 的重点是从“会做”提升到“会判断”。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 18 is about moving from simply performing tasks to making sound clinical judgments.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 clinical decisions 不能只靠固定模板？
questions.questionEn: Why can’t clinical decisions rely only on fixed templates?

options.zh: 因为病人病情、背景和风险不同，需要结合情境判断
options.en: Because patient condition, context, and risk differ and require situational judgment

options.zh: 因为模板永远错误
options.en: Because templates are always wrong

options.zh: 因为 assessment 不重要
options.en: Because assessment is unimportant

options.zh: 因为所有病人都一样
options.en: Because all patients are the same

questions.explanationZh: 临床判断要把流程、病情严重度和背景整合起来。
questions.explanationEn: Clinical judgment requires integrating process, acuity, and context.

questions.questionZh: 在 clinical decision-making 中，最关键的问题之一通常是什么？
questions.questionEn: In clinical decision-making, what is often one of the most important questions?

options.zh: 这个病人 sick 还是 not sick
options.en: Whether this patient is sick or not sick

options.zh: 病人喜欢哪种医院颜色
options.en: Which hospital color the patient prefers

options.zh: 今天值班几个人
options.en: How many staff are on shift today

options.zh: 病人有没有看过这本书
options.en: Whether the patient has read the textbook

questions.explanationZh: 很多院前判断都先从严重程度分层开始。
questions.explanationEn: Many prehospital decisions begin with recognizing the patient’s level of acuity.

questions.questionZh: clinical decisions 与 protocols 的关系哪项更准确？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best describes the relationship between clinical decisions and protocols?

options.zh: protocols 提供框架，但仍需要临床判断来应用
options.en: Protocols provide a framework, but clinical judgment is still needed to apply them

options.zh: 只要有 protocol 就不需要思考
options.en: If a protocol exists, no thinking is required

options.zh: clinical judgment 可以完全忽视 protocol
options.en: Clinical judgment can completely ignore protocols

options.zh: 两者互不相关
options.en: They are unrelated

questions.explanationZh: protocol 不是替代思考，而是帮助结构化地做判断。
questions.explanationEn: Protocols do not replace thinking; they support structured decision-making.

questions.questionZh: Communications and Documentation 这一章的核心作用是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core role of the Communications and Documentation chapter?

options.zh: 把临床信息正确传达、交班并记录下来
options.en: To communicate, hand over, and document clinical information accurately

options.zh: 只学习无线电品牌
options.en: To learn radio brand names only

options.zh: 替代 patient assessment
options.en: To replace patient assessment

options.zh: 只服务 billing
options.en: To serve billing only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 19 强调 clinical work 只有被正确传达和记录，才真正完整。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 19 emphasizes that clinical work is only complete when it is accurately communicated and documented.

questions.questionZh: radio communication 在 EMS 中的重要性最贴近哪项？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best describes the importance of radio communication in EMS?

options.zh: 它帮助提前通知、协调资源并改善交接连续性
options.en: It helps with prearrival notification, resource coordination, and continuity of handoff

options.zh: 它主要只是为了聊天
options.en: It is mainly for casual conversation

options.zh: 它只在 training 中用
options.en: It is only used in training

options.zh: 它和 patient care 没关系
options.en: It has nothing to do with patient care

questions.explanationZh: 良好的 radio communication 直接影响接收方准备和系统协作。
questions.explanationEn: Good radio communication directly affects receiving-facility preparedness and system coordination.

questions.questionZh: 哪种 documentation 原则最正确？
questions.questionEn: Which documentation principle is most correct?

options.zh: 准确、相关、清晰并与实际发现一致
options.en: It should be accurate, relevant, clear, and consistent with actual findings

options.zh: 只写最少几个字
options.en: Write as little as possible

options.zh: 把未评估的内容也写成正常
options.en: Document unassessed findings as normal

options.zh: 发现和记录不一致也没关系
options.en: It is fine if the record does not match the findings

questions.explanationZh: 优秀 documentation 的基础就是准确、清楚、可追溯。
questions.explanationEn: Good documentation must be accurate, clear, and defensible.

questions.questionZh: handoff communication 最重要的目标之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most important goals of handoff communication?

options.zh: 让接手团队快速理解病人状态、你做过什么以及接下来要注意什么
options.en: To help the receiving team quickly understand the patient’s condition, what was done, and what to watch next

options.zh: 尽量缩短到听不懂也没关系
options.en: To make it so short that details no longer matter

options.zh: 只讲个人感受
options.en: To focus only on personal impressions

options.zh: 避免提任何异常
options.en: To avoid mentioning any abnormalities

questions.explanationZh: handoff 的重点是关键信息的连续传递，而不是形式完成。
questions.explanationEn: The point of a handoff is continuity of essential information, not just completing a formality.

questions.questionZh: Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury 这一章的核心能力是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the key skill taught in Trauma Systems and Mechanism of Injury?

options.zh: 从受伤机制推测潜在损伤
options.en: Inferring likely injuries from the mechanism of injury

options.zh: 只背医院电话
options.en: Memorizing hospital phone numbers

options.zh: 只学药理学
options.en: Studying pharmacology only

options.zh: 只学文件收费
options.en: Learning documentation billing only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 20 的核心就是把动力学和能量传递转化成损伤推理。
questions.explanationEn: The core of Chapter 20 is turning kinematics and energy transfer into injury prediction.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 mechanism of injury 对 trauma assessment 很重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is mechanism of injury important in trauma assessment?

options.zh: 因为它帮助预测看不见的内部伤
options.en: Because it helps predict unseen internal injuries

options.zh: 因为它能取代全部查体
options.en: Because it replaces the physical exam

options.zh: 因为它只用于保险理赔
options.en: Because it is only for insurance claims

options.zh: 因为创伤里病史不重要
options.en: Because history is unimportant in trauma

questions.explanationZh: MOI 帮助你在还没完全看见伤口前先建立“哪里可能坏了”的模型。
questions.explanationEn: MOI helps build an early model of what may be injured before everything is visibly obvious.

questions.questionZh: 高能量创伤机制最应提醒你什么？
questions.questionEn: What should a high-energy trauma mechanism most strongly remind you of?

options.zh: 可能存在严重或多系统损伤
options.en: The possibility of serious or multisystem injury

options.zh: 病人一定没事
options.en: That the patient is definitely fine

options.zh: 只需处理表浅擦伤
options.en: That only superficial abrasions matter

options.zh: 可以忽略生命体征
options.en: That vital signs can be ignored

questions.explanationZh: 高能量传递常意味着更深层、更广泛的损伤风险。
questions.explanationEn: High-energy transfer often means a greater risk of deeper and wider injury.

questions.questionZh: trauma systems 的主要价值之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one major value of trauma systems?

options.zh: 让病人更快到合适层级的 definitive care
options.en: Getting patients to the right level of definitive care more efficiently

options.zh: 让所有病人都去同一家医院
options.en: Sending every patient to the same hospital

options.zh: 减少 assessment 的需要
options.en: Reducing the need for assessment

options.zh: 只为了 paperwork
options.en: Only for paperwork

questions.explanationZh: 创伤系统的意义在于分流、资源匹配和提高救治效率。
questions.explanationEn: Trauma systems matter because of triage, resource matching, and improved treatment efficiency.

questions.questionZh: Bleeding and Shock 这一章最核心的危险主线是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the most important danger pathway in Bleeding and Shock?

options.zh: 失血导致灌注不足和休克
options.en: Blood loss leading to poor perfusion and shock

options.zh: 轻度皮疹
options.en: A mild rash

options.zh: 单纯关节僵硬
options.en: Isolated joint stiffness

options.zh: 文书延迟
options.en: Delayed paperwork

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 21 的核心就是出血、灌注失败和休克进展。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 21 centers on bleeding, perfusion failure, and progression into shock.

questions.questionZh: 在 trauma 病人里，为什么早期识别 shock 很重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is early recognition of shock so important in trauma patients?

options.zh: 因为它直接关系到生存和处理优先级
options.en: Because it directly affects survival and treatment priorities

options.zh: 因为 shock 总会自己恢复
options.en: Because shock always resolves on its own

options.zh: 因为只有医院才需要关心 shock
options.en: Because only hospitals need to worry about shock

options.zh: 因为它与出血无关
options.en: Because it has nothing to do with bleeding

questions.explanationZh: 休克的早期识别会改变出血控制、转运速度和整体策略。
questions.explanationEn: Early shock recognition changes hemorrhage control, transport urgency, and the overall strategy.

questions.questionZh: 在 bleeding and shock 的学习逻辑里，哪项常常是致命关键？
questions.questionEn: In the logic of Bleeding and Shock, what is often a lethal key issue?

options.zh: 持续未控制的出血
options.en: Ongoing uncontrolled hemorrhage

options.zh: 旧瘀青颜色
options.en: The color of an old bruise

options.zh: 病人喜欢喝什么
options.en: What the patient likes to drink

options.zh: 手套尺码
options.en: Glove size

questions.explanationZh: 未控制出血会持续破坏灌注，是最应优先处理的问题之一。
questions.explanationEn: Uncontrolled hemorrhage continues to destroy perfusion and is one of the highest-priority problems to address.

questions.questionZh: 为什么骨盆创伤在这章里特别重要？
questions.questionEn: Why are pelvic injuries especially important in this chapter?

options.zh: 因为它们可能伴随大量隐匿性失血
options.en: Because they may involve major hidden blood loss

options.zh: 因为它们通常只是小伤
options.en: Because they are usually minor injuries

options.zh: 因为它们不会影响灌注
options.en: Because they do not affect perfusion

options.zh: 因为它们只影响 documentation
options.en: Because they only affect documentation

questions.explanationZh: 骨盆损伤常是创伤休克和隐匿性大出血的重要来源。
questions.explanationEn: Pelvic injuries are a major source of hidden hemorrhage and traumatic shock.

questions.questionZh: Soft-Tissue Injuries 这一章的重点之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one major focus of Soft-Tissue Injuries?

options.zh: 识别和处理开放/闭合软组织损伤
options.en: Recognizing and managing open and closed soft-tissue injuries

options.zh: 只讨论 ECG
options.en: Discussing ECG only

options.zh: 只讨论法律案例
options.en: Discussing legal cases only

options.zh: 只讨论儿科沟通
options.en: Discussing pediatric communication only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 22 聚焦开放伤、闭合伤、挤压伤和爆炸伤等软组织问题。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 22 focuses on open and closed wounds, crush injuries, blast injuries, and related soft-tissue problems.

questions.questionZh: 为什么 soft-tissue injury 容易被低估？
questions.questionEn: Why are soft-tissue injuries easy to underestimate?

options.zh: 因为外表看起来不大时，深层损伤或失血仍可能严重
options.en: Because deep injury or blood loss may still be significant even when the outside looks limited

options.zh: 因为软组织永远不会严重
options.en: Because soft tissue is never serious

options.zh: 因为它们只影响皮肤颜色
options.en: Because they only affect skin color

options.zh: 因为只要包扎就结束了
options.en: Because bandaging always solves everything

questions.explanationZh: 软组织伤的外观不一定能反映全部严重程度。
questions.explanationEn: The external appearance of a soft-tissue injury does not always reflect its true severity.

questions.questionZh: 爆炸伤（blast injuries）为什么值得单独重视？
questions.questionEn: Why do blast injuries deserve special attention?

options.zh: 因为它们可能造成多机制、多系统损伤
options.en: Because they can produce multi-mechanism, multisystem injury

options.zh: 因为它们只会造成皮肤划伤
options.en: Because they only cause skin scratches

options.zh: 因为它们和 trauma systems 无关
options.en: Because they are unrelated to trauma systems

options.zh: 因为它们不需要 assessment
options.en: Because they do not need assessment

questions.explanationZh: blast injuries 可能同时涉及冲击波、碎片、抛掷和烧伤等机制。
questions.explanationEn: Blast injuries may involve blast-wave effects, fragments, body displacement, burns, and more.

questions.questionZh: 处理大型软组织伤时，一个常见目标是什么？
questions.questionEn: When managing major soft-tissue injury, what is a common goal?

options.zh: 控制出血、保护组织并减少进一步损害
options.en: To control bleeding, protect tissue, and limit further harm

options.zh: 尽量多移动伤处
options.en: To move the injured area as much as possible

options.zh: 延后所有评估
options.en: To delay all assessment

options.zh: 优先讨论出院计划
options.en: To prioritize discharge planning

questions.explanationZh: 软组织伤处理通常围绕 hemorrhage control、protection 和 reassessment。
questions.explanationEn: Management usually centers on hemorrhage control, tissue protection, and reassessment.

questions.questionZh: Burns 这一章最想纠正什么误区？
questions.questionEn: What major misconception does the Burns chapter try to correct?

options.zh: 烧伤不只是皮肤问题，而可能是全身性问题
options.en: That burns are not just skin problems but can be systemic problems

options.zh: 所有烧伤都不痛
options.en: That burns are always painless

options.zh: 烧伤与液体无关
options.en: That burns have nothing to do with fluids

options.zh: 烧伤都不需要转运
options.en: That burns never require transport

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 23 强调烧伤可能影响气道、循环、液体平衡和整体生理。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 23 emphasizes that burns can affect the airway, circulation, fluid balance, and whole-body physiology.

questions.questionZh: 为什么烧伤评估不能只看表面大小？
questions.questionEn: Why can’t burn assessment rely only on the visible surface size?

options.zh: 因为深度、位置、吸入伤和全身反应同样重要
options.en: Because depth, location, inhalation injury, and systemic response also matter

options.zh: 因为大小完全不重要
options.en: Because size does not matter at all

options.zh: 因为所有烧伤处理都一样
options.en: Because all burns are treated exactly the same

options.zh: 因为烧伤只看年龄
options.en: Because burns are assessed only by age

questions.explanationZh: 烧伤评估需要综合深度、面积、部位和并发风险。
questions.explanationEn: Burn assessment must integrate depth, size, location, and associated risks.

questions.questionZh: 为什么某些烧伤病人需要考虑烧伤中心？
questions.questionEn: Why should some burn patients be considered for transfer to a burn center?

options.zh: 因为特定烧伤需要更高层级的专业处理
options.en: Because certain burns require a higher level of specialized care

options.zh: 因为所有烧伤都必须去最远的医院
options.en: Because every burn must go to the farthest hospital

options.zh: 因为一般医院不能包扎
options.en: Because general hospitals cannot apply dressings

options.zh: 因为烧伤只由外科决定
options.en: Because burns are decided by surgeons alone

questions.explanationZh: 重度、特殊部位或复杂烧伤常需要 specialized burn care。
questions.explanationEn: Severe, complex, or special-location burns often require specialized burn-center care.

questions.questionZh: 烧伤病人的一个重要临床关注点是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is an important clinical concern in burn patients?

options.zh: 液体丢失和持续生理恶化风险
options.en: The risk of fluid loss and ongoing physiologic deterioration

options.zh: 病人是否喜欢凉水
options.en: Whether the patient likes cool water

options.zh: 他们通常不会有 airway 问题
options.en: That they usually will not have airway issues

options.zh: 只需要处理疼痛，不用考虑别的
options.en: That pain is the only issue to address

questions.explanationZh: 烧伤常伴随液体转移、休克风险、疼痛和 airway concerns。
questions.explanationEn: Burns often involve fluid shifts, shock risk, pain, and airway concerns.

questions.questionZh: Head and Face Injuries 为什么在院前环境中特别敏感？
questions.questionEn: Why are head and face injuries especially sensitive in the prehospital setting?

options.zh: 因为它们常同时牵涉 airway、neuro 和 facial trauma 问题
options.en: Because they often involve airway, neuro, and facial-trauma issues at the same time

options.zh: 因为它们永远都是轻伤
options.en: Because they are always minor injuries

options.zh: 因为它们只影响外观
options.en: Because they only affect appearance

options.zh: 因为它们从不需要 transport
options.en: Because they never require transport

questions.explanationZh: 颅面部损伤容易同时影响气道、意识、出血和感觉器官。
questions.explanationEn: Head and face injuries can simultaneously affect the airway, consciousness, bleeding, and sensory organs.

questions.questionZh: 评估 head injury 时，一个高优先级问题是什么？
questions.questionEn: When assessing head injury, what is a high-priority question?

options.zh: 是否存在 brain injury 或 neurologic deterioration 迹象
options.en: Whether there are signs of brain injury or neurologic deterioration

options.zh: 头发长度多少
options.en: How long the patient’s hair is

options.zh: 平时戴不戴帽子
options.en: Whether the patient usually wears a hat

options.zh: 喜欢哪种眼镜品牌
options.en: What eyewear brand the patient likes

questions.explanationZh: 头部损伤 assessment 的重点之一是识别 TBI 和神经恶化。
questions.explanationEn: A major goal in head-injury assessment is recognizing TBI and neurologic deterioration.

questions.questionZh: face injury 为什么可能很快变成 airway problem？
questions.questionEn: Why can a face injury quickly become an airway problem?

options.zh: 因为出血、肿胀和结构损伤可能威胁气道通畅
options.en: Because bleeding, swelling, and structural injury can threaten airway patency

options.zh: 因为所有面部伤都会造成胸痛
options.en: Because all facial injuries cause chest pain

options.zh: 因为面部损伤只影响说话，不影响气道
options.en: Because facial injuries affect speech only, not the airway

options.zh: 因为面部损伤不能出血
options.en: Because facial injuries cannot bleed

questions.explanationZh: 面部外伤常通过出血、 swelling 和解剖变形影响 airway。
questions.explanationEn: Facial trauma can compromise the airway through bleeding, swelling, and anatomic distortion.

questions.questionZh: Head and Face Injuries 这一章为什么也会涉及眼耳口腔与颈前部？
questions.questionEn: Why does the Head and Face Injuries chapter also include the eyes, ears, mouth, and anterior neck?

options.zh: 因为这些区域彼此相邻，损伤常互相影响并影响 airway/neurologic assessment
options.en: Because these adjacent regions influence one another and can affect airway and neurologic assessment

options.zh: 因为只是为了让章节更长
options.en: Only to make the chapter longer

options.zh: 因为它们与 face 完全无关
options.en: Because they are completely unrelated to the face

options.zh: 因为这些部位不需要检查
options.en: Because these areas do not need to be examined

questions.explanationZh: 头面部评估常需要一并考虑眼耳口鼻和颈前结构。
questions.explanationEn: Head-and-face assessment often requires integrated evaluation of the eyes, ears, mouth, nose, and anterior neck structures.
